Advodaza.name: Advodaza
Advodaza.publicNotes: These devils prey upon the superstitious, masquerading as lost or obscure divinities, and ultimately corrupt whatever cults they rule.
Advodaza.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Idol Armor.name: Idol Armor
action->Idol Armor.description: The advodaza's defiled idols grant them resistance 15 to two damage types of their choice except fire, physical, poison, and spirit (typically mental and void). The advodaza can spend 10 minutes to change which damage types are resisted. Any holy effect that affects the fiend can also counteract their idol armor (DC 40), which the advodaza can reactivate with a Sustain action.

action->False Divinity.name: False Divinity
action->False Divinity.description:
The advodaza chooses two domains, typically chosen from the following list: ambition, darkness, fire, might, trickery, tyranny, wealth, or zeal. The advodaza gains both domains’ initial domain spells as well as the advanced domain spell from one of the domains, all heightened to 9th rank.

Note: For the purposes of Foundry Data Entry, fire and might were the domains chosen. Change the focus spells the Advodaza knows for the domains of your choice.

action->Infernal Mark.name: Infernal Mark
action->Infernal Mark.description: The advodaza touches a willing creature and marks it with a glyph that functions as a 9th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.AUctDF2fqPZN2w4W]{Sigil}, which the devil can make visible or invisible. The target gains 1 Focus Point if it didn't already have any, and the advodaza grants it one of their initial domain spells as a focus spell. The marked creature and the advodaza can communicate telepathically with each other while on the same plane. The advodaza can maintain up to 10 Infernal Marks at a time; if they create another after reaching this limit, the oldest mark is Dismissed. The advodaza can Dismiss any number of Infernal Marks with a single action.

action->Punish Disciple.name: Punish Disciple
action->Punish Disciple.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The advodaza chooses up to three creatures bearing their Infernal Mark that are on the same plane as the advodaza, dealing @Damage[8d6[spirit]] damage to each creature (@Check[fortitude|dc:40|basic] save).

Alcabanoth.name: Alcabanoth
Alcabanoth.publicNotes: These devils prey upon the superstitious, masquerading as lost or obscure divinities, and ultimately corrupt whatever cults they rule.
Alcabanoth.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Idol Armor.name: Idol Armor
action->Idol Armor.description: The advodaza's defiled idols grant them resistance 15 to two damage types of their choice except fire, physical, poison, and spirit (typically mental and void). The advodaza can spend 10 minutes to change which damage types are resisted. Any holy effect that affects the fiend can also counteract their idol armor (DC 40), which the advodaza can reactivate with a Sustain action.

action->False Divinity.name: False Divinity
action->False Divinity.description:
Alcabanoth has access to the ambition and zeal domains.

The advodaza chooses two domains, typically chosen from the following list: ambition, darkness, fire, might, trickery, tyranny, wealth, or zeal. The advodaza gains both domains’ initial domain spells as well as the advanced domain spell from one of the domains, all heightened to 9th rank.

action->Infernal Mark.name: Infernal Mark
action->Infernal Mark.description: The advodaza touches a willing creature and marks it with a glyph that functions as a 9th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.AUctDF2fqPZN2w4W]{Sigil}, which the devil can make visible or invisible. The target gains 1 Focus Point if it didn't already have any, and the advodaza grants it one of their initial domain spells as a focus spell. The marked creature and the advodaza can communicate telepathically with each other while on the same plane. The advodaza can maintain up to 10 Infernal Marks at a time; if they create another after reaching this limit, the oldest mark is Dismissed. The advodaza can Dismiss any number of Infernal Marks with a single action.

action->Punish Disciple.name: Punish Disciple
action->Punish Disciple.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The advodaza chooses up to three creatures bearing their Infernal Mark that are on the same plane as the advodaza, dealing @Damage[8d6[spirit]] damage to each creature (@Check[fortitude|dc:40|basic] save).

Alchemical Cloud.name: Alchemical Cloud
Alchemical Cloud.descriptionHazard: The collection of bottles at the bottom of one locker have been jostled. Their contents leak into a puddle, and their vapors burn through the open air.
Alchemical Cloud.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:30] (master) to neutralize the alchemical substances, or @Check[thievery|dc:35] (expert) to carefully clean the spill
Alchemical Cloud.reset: If the reagents aren't cleaned up before closing the locker, the accumulating gases reset the trap after 1 hour.

action->Leaking Reagents.name: Leaking Reagents
action->Leaking Reagents.description:
Trigger A creature fully opens the locker containing the alchemical clutter

Effect A cloud of vapor deals @Damage[8d10[poison]] damage to all creatures within 10 feet (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic] save). On a failure, a target takes an additional @Damage[2d10[persistent,poison]] damage.

action->Aura of Righteousness.name: Aura of Righteousness
action->Aura of Righteousness.description: 20 feet. Allies in the angelic host's aura gain a +2 status bonus to AC and saving throws against unholy creatures.

action->Blessed Armaments.name: Blessed Armaments
action->Blessed Armaments.description:
The angels make coordinated melee attacks with their greatswords. Each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:39|basic|traits:holy,magical] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[2d8[slashing],(1d6)[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d8+8)[slashing],(2d6)[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(6d8+10)[slashing],(3d6)[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Divine Lance Fusillade.name: Divine Lance Fusillade
action->Divine Lance Fusillade.description: The angels unleash beams of divine energy in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet that deals @Damage[10d6[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:39|basic|traits:holy,magical] save. When the angelic host is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Troop Spellcasting.name: Troop Spellcasting
action->Troop Spellcasting.description: When the angelic host Casts a healing Spell, the constituent members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version of the spell than any one member could achieve alone. The spell restores an additional 2 Hit Points per rank.

action->Eternal Luminosity.name: Eternal Luminosity
action->Eternal Luminosity.description: An anguished hellfire naturally sheds brilliant light like a torch. When other creatures target the anguished hellfire, they ignore the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} condition from darkness, fog, mist, and smoke.

action->Solar Flare.name: Solar Flare
action->Solar Flare.description: 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:38|basic|traits:divine,visual] save). On a failed save, it also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} until the end of its next turn. The anguished hellfire can deactivate or activate this aura by using a single action that has the concentrate trait.

action->Vulnerable to Blasphemy.name: Vulnerable to Blasphemy
action->Vulnerable to Blasphemy.description: If a creature the anguished flame can see and hear spends 1 action with the linguistic trait blaspheming Asmodeus, the anguished flame becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} until they Collect a Prayer from that creature.

action->Collect Unholy Prayer.name: Collect Unholy Prayer
action->Collect Unholy Prayer.description:
The anguished flame compels the prayers of a creature they can see within 60 feet, and that creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:41|traits:emotion,mental] save. A creature with the holy trait gets a degree of success one better than their result.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature uses its reaction to pray.

Failure The creature prays. It is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use reactions for 1 minute.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2}.

action->Focus Gaze.name: Focus Gaze
action->Focus Gaze.description:
Requirements The anguished hellfire's solar flare aura is active

Effect The anguished hellfire fixes their fiery eyes on a creature they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Fortitude save against the anguished hellfire's solar flare. If the creature was already @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} by solar flare before attempting its save, a failed save causes it to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} until the end of its next turn.

action->Scourging Flame.name: Scourging Flame
action->Scourging Flame.description: The hellfire can burn away beneficial magic. It attempts a flaming wing Strike. On a success, the anguished hellfire attempts to counteract one spell effect of its choice active on the target. The counteract modifier is +36, and the counteract rank is 9th.

Animated Cage Trap.name: Animated Cage Trap
Animated Cage Trap.descriptionHazard: A spiky iron cage pulls in a victim with animated chains before slamming shut and locking tight.
Animated Cage Trap.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:45] (master) to Pick the Lock, @Check[athletics|dc:48] (master) to break the lock or bend the bars; five total successes are required to disable the trap
Animated Cage Trap.reset: The trap deactivates 1 minute after there are no longer any potential prisoners in the room and then activates 1 round after that.
Animated Cage Trap.routine: (3 actions) As 1 action, the trap makes a chain Strike against a target in its reach; its Athletics modifier for Improved Grab is +36. The trap can have any number of creatures @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by its chains. If the trap has any creatures grabbed or restrained, it attempts to pull them closer as 1 action with an Athletics check against the creature's Fortitude DC; on a success, it pulls the creature 5 feet closer (10 feet on a critical success). A Large or smaller creature can be pulled inside the cage, after which the cage door slams shut. The cage can only contain one creature, and that creature is restrained while inside ([[/act escape dc=50]]). As 1 action, the trap can Constrict the creature inside the cage with more chains.
Animated Cage Trap.stealth: (master)

action->Capturing Chain.name: Capturing Chain
action->Capturing Chain.description:
Trigger A creature that isn't a member of the inquisition or an authorized ally comes within 15 feet of the cage

Effect The trap makes a chain Strike against the triggering creature. If the trap damages and successfully grapples the target with Improved Grab, the trap pulls the target 5 feet closer (or into the cage if the Strike was a critical hit). The trap then rolls initiative.

Aquatic Viper Vine.name: Aquatic Viper Vine
Aquatic Viper Vine.publicNotes:
A voracious, flesh-eating carnivore, the viper vine has a single enormous blossom arising from a thick, leafy tangle of snakelike vines. When the plant senses the approach of suitable prey through its sensitive, shallow-buried root system, it rises up like an agitated snake and unfurls its brightly colored bloom, an act that releases a cloud of mind-numbing pollen.

Since viper vines gain nourishment by consuming creatures rather than through photosynthesis and absorbing nutrients from the soil, they have developed rudimentary locomotion and can drag themselves along the ground with tentacle-like roots. They even have a form of rudimentary sentience, allowing them to both discern differences in prey and make limited tactical decisions, while also avoiding creatures that are particularly large or dangerous looking.

The area around viper vine hunting grounds is often strewn with the partially devoured remains of victims. It's not unusual to find the rotting corpses of wild animals, ill-fated adventurers, and even giants in the plant's immediate vicinity, along with a scattering of incidental treasure left behind on the corpses. A viper vine rarely returns to the carcass of a creature it killed earlier, preferring to hunt fresh meat.

action->Cold Vulnerability.name: Cold Vulnerability
action->Cold Vulnerability.description: When exposed to a cold effect, the viper vine is overwhelmed by lethargy, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Captivating Pollen.name: Captivating Pollen
action->Captivating Pollen.description:
The viper vine releases a @Template[type:emanation|distance:60] of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} pollen that stays in the air for 5 rounds unless dispersed by a moderate or stronger wind. Each creature that enters or starts its turn in the area must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:33] save or be captivated. The viper vine can't use Captivating Pollen for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Captivating Pollen for 24 hours.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated}, and it must spend each of its actions to move closer to the viper vine as expediently as possible while avoiding obvious dangers. If a captivated creature is adjacent to the viper vine, it stays still and doesn't act. It ceases to be fascinated if it's no longer in the pollen aura at the end of its turn.

Critical Failure As failure, plus the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 24 hours.

action->Construct Armor (Hardness 10).name: Construct Armor (Hardness 10)
action->Construct Armor (Hardness 10).description: Like normal objects, the archheathen's fool has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once the archheathen's fool is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks, removing the Hardness and reducing its Armor Class to 34.

action->Trickery Vulnerability.name: Trickery Vulnerability
action->Trickery Vulnerability.description: The effigy is easily tricked. It isn't immune to attempts to [[/act create-a-diversion]] or [[/act feint]]. If a creature succeeds at either action, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round as it processes the trickery. Each additional success increases its slowed condition by 1.

weapon->Mace.name: Mace
weapon->Mace.description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and allows its wielder to deliver powerful blows and dent armor.

armor->Breastplate.name: Breastplate
armor->Breastplate.description: Though referred to as a breastplate, this type of armor consists of several pieces of plate or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.pRoikbRo5HFW6YUB]{Half Plate} armor that protect the torso, chest, neck, and sometimes the hips and lower legs. It strategically grants some of the protection of plate while allowing greater flexibility and speed.

action->Cast Down in Flames.name: Cast Down in Flames
action->Cast Down in Flames.description: Aspexia brings the sheer force of her faith crashing down on a foe. If the next action she takes is to cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} to damage one creature, the target is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} if it takes any damage from the spell. If the target fails its saving throw against the spell, it also takes @Damage[3d6[persistent,fire]] damage.

action->Channel Smite.name: Channel Smite
action->Channel Smite.description:
Requirements Aspexia expends a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} spell

Effect Aspexia makes a melee Strike. If it hits, she damages the target with a 1-action harm spell, The target automatically gets a failure (or a critical failure if the Strike was a critical hit). The spell doesn't have the manipulate trait when cast this way.

action->Deadly Simplicity.name: Deadly Simplicity
action->Deadly Simplicity.description: Aspexia deals 1d8 damage with her mace instead of 1d6.

action->Rebounding Smite.name: Rebounding Smite
action->Rebounding Smite.description:
Trigger Aspexia misses with her Strike during Channel Smite

Effect Aspexia retains the energy from her smite. She Casts the 1-action version of the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} spell she expended, targeting a creature other than the target of her Strike.

action->Unholy Bloodshed.name: Unholy Bloodshed
action->Unholy Bloodshed.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger Aspexia critically succeeds at an attack roll against a holy creature with her Unholy Mace

Effect The target takes @Damage[3d8[bleed]].

Athervox.name: Athervox
Athervox.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. primal

action->Camouflage.name: Camouflage
action->Camouflage.description: Athervox can [[/act hide]] in natural environments even if she doesn't have cover.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Prima.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Primal

action->Miasma.name: Miasma
action->Miasma.description:
20 feet.

After Athervox uses her Poison Breath, a cloud of poison gas continues to emanate from her body for 1 round. Any creature that ends their turn in the miasma takes @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:39|basic] save). Any creature in the miasma is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} and treats other creatures as concealed. Athervox can see through this concealment.

action->Thrusting Tail.name: Thrusting Tail
action->Thrusting Tail.description:
Trigger A creature within reach of Athervox's tail Casts a Spell

Effect Athervox makes a tail Strike at the creature with a –2 penalty. If the Strike hits, Athervox disrupts the creature's action.

action->Warding Focus.name: Warding Focus
action->Warding Focus.description: Spells that Athervox Casts that target herself have their durations doubled.

action->Forest Passage.name: Forest Passage
action->Forest Passage.description: Athervox ignores all difficult terrain caused by plants, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth. Even plants manipulated by magic doesn't impede her progress.

action->Impaling Charge.name: Impaling Charge
action->Impaling Charge.description:
Requirements Athervox doesn't have a creature impaled on her horn

Effect Athervox attempts to gore a foe. She Strides, then attempts a horn Strike. On a hit, the target becomes impaled on Athervox's horn. The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} while on the horn (and can Escape as normal). Athervox doesn't need to use additional actions to keep the impaled creature grabbed. If Athervox moves, she brings the grabbed creature along with her.

action->Poison Breath.name: Poison Breath
action->Poison Breath.description: Athervox breathes a toxic cloud that deals @Damage[19d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:60] (@Check[fortitude|dc:39|basic] save) that doesn't damage objects. She can't use Poison Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Poison Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Telescopic Scrying.name: Telescopic Scrying
action->Telescopic Scrying.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect When peering through a telescope, Athervox can survey and monitor her surroundings as well as astronomical phenomena. She can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4WAib3GichxLjp5p]{Alarm} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Ek5XI0aEdZhBgm21]{Scouting Eye}, each with a maximum range of 5 miles from the telescope used as a focus.

action->Trackless Journey.name: Trackless Journey
action->Trackless Journey.description: Athervox always gains the benefit of Cover Tracks in natural surroundings, even while moving at full speed.

Avernal Vortex.name: Avernal Vortex
Avernal Vortex.descriptionHazard: The edges of the extraplanar tunnel seem to writhe with malevolence, as if preventing unwanted souls from passing through.
Avernal Vortex.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:50] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:50] (master) to allow the group to traverse along the portal tunnel; six total successes are required to complete the journey; a PC who succeeds at a @Check[acrobatics|dc:48], @Check[athletics|dc:48], or @Check[survival|dc:48] check as a 2-action activity grants themselves a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and saves against the hazard's routine for 1 round (on a critical success, they can also grant this bonus to one other PC)
Avernal Vortex.reset: Once the PCs pass through the portal tunnel and arrive in Hell, the Avernal vortex ends and doesn't reset.
Avernal Vortex.routine:
(1 action; divine) The Avernal vortex churns across the portal tunnel, stopping the PCs from reaching their ultimate destination. In the first round, the vortex takes an action to attempt to dispel all beneficial spells on all creatures in the tunnel, scouring them with Hellfire. The vortex has a counteract modifier of [[/r 1d20+43 #Counteract]]{+43} and a counteract rank of 10, and rolls separately for each spell. On a success, the target creature takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:45|basic] save (this has the divine, fire, and unholy traits).

In the second round, the vortex attempts to instill a dread of tumbling forever through the tunnel to those within it; each creature takes @Damage[6d6[persistent,mental]] damage with a @Check[will|dc:45|basic] save (this is an emotion and mental effect). Once a creature ends its persistent mental damage (or if they avoided the persistent damage), they can reach and escape the tunnel.

In all subsequent rounds, the vortex releases bolts of acid, cold, fire, and electricity. It makes one vortex bolt Strike against each creature in the tunnel.

action->Gaze of Barbatos.name: Gaze of Barbatos
action->Gaze of Barbatos.description:
Trigger A living creature enters the portal tunnel

Effect As the PCs begin to pierce planar boundaries, they feel they've caught the attention of the archdevil Barbatos, ruler of Avernus. Each creature in the tunnel must attempt a @Check[will|dc:45|traits:divine,emotion,fear,fire,mental,unholy] save. The hazard then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 minute and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3} for 1 minute and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}.

strike-melee->Vortex Bolt.name: Vortex Bolt
strike-melee->Vortex Bolt.description: acid, cold, fire, or electricity; the damage is chosen randomly, and the Strike gains the appropriate trait; no multiple attack penalty

Bdellavritra.name: Bdellavritra
Bdellavritra.publicNotes: Masters of possession, bdellavritras enact complex schemes through pawns in the mortal world. Even if expelled from their hosts, these fiends retain fearsome physical strength with bodies that bely their agility.
Bdellavritra.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->Mental Synergy.name: Mental Synergy
action->Mental Synergy.description:
Frequency once per minute

Requirements The bdellavritra is possessing a host using Mind Worm

Effect If the bdellavritra's next action is to Cast a Spell, they reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).

action->Mind Worm.name: Mind Worm
action->Mind Worm.description: The bdellavritra spends 10 minutes adjacent to an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} or willing living creature, then attempts to possess its body. This functions as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wU6hNzK8Yfqdmc8m]{Possession} heightened to 9th rank (@Check[will|dc:37] save), except that it has a duration of 1 month. If affected, the host receives new saving throws only at the start of each day. If the host dies, the bdellavritra must attempt a Fortitude save as normal but is only @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} on a failure (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 4} on a critical failure).

action->Pleasant Voices.name: Pleasant Voices
action->Pleasant Voices.description:
Requirements The bdellavritra isn't possessing a host

Effect One of the bdellavritra's mouths sings to a living creature within 30 feet. Unless the target succeeds at a @Check[will|dc:37] save (with a +4 circumstance bonus if the devil or their allies recently threatened the target), they can't use hostile actions against the bdellavritra for 1 round. The bdellavritra can Sustain this effect for up to 1 hour; each time they do, they can affect an additional target (maximum four targets). Each affected target can attempt a new saving throw to end the effect whenever they can't hear the bdellavritra for 1 round or the devil uses a hostile action against one of them.

Bellflower Chant.name: Bellflower Chant
Bellflower Chant.descriptionHazard: A chorus of Bellflower Network agents and allies chant about freedom from tyranny.
Bellflower Chant.disable: @Check[performance|dc:21] (trained) to join the chant, or @Check[society|dc:25] to identify the Bellflower agents' tactics
Bellflower Chant.routine: (1 action) The Bellflower chant continues their song. All non–Bellflower Network creatures in area A must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save. On a failure, the target is offguard until the end of its next turn.

action->Find Freedom in the Song.name: Find Freedom in the Song
action->Find Freedom in the Song.description:
Trigger Zefiro becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}

Effect The Bellflower chant swells from the assembled crowd. All grabbed, restrained, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} creatures can attempt to Escape as a free action. The Bellflower chant then rolls initiative.

Brimstone Corps.name: Brimstone Corps
Brimstone Corps.publicNotes: Soldiers who participate in multiple campaigns of war gain the experience necessary to become their military's finest fighting forces. First-class infantry troops are deployed on battlefields where winning the day is of the utmost importance.

action->Hell's Call.name: Hell's Call
action->Hell's Call.description: The Brimstone Corps have pledged their souls to Hell and welcome its embrace. The troop doesn't attempt a rout check when it's reduced from one HP threshold to another. When reduced to 2 segments, the troop gains a +2 circumstance bonus to attack and damage rolls as the last surviving troop members sell their lives dearly before they're called to Hell.

action->No Retreat.name: No Retreat
action->No Retreat.description: These soldiers have been extensively trained to hold their ground no matter the situation. If any effect would force the brimstone corps to move, reduce the distance by 10 feet. Any time they would be affected by the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition, the brimstone corps is instead @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for the same duration.

action->Bolt Salvo.name: Bolt Salvo
action->Bolt Salvo.description: The brimstone corps draws, loads, and shoots a salvo from their crossbows. The salvo is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 120 feet that deals @Damage[4d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save). When the brimstone corps is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Clash of Steel.name: Clash of Steel
action->Clash of Steel.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The brimstone corps attacks each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(2d6+1)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d6+10)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(4d6+18)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Drilled in Formations.name: Drilled in Formations
action->Drilled in Formations.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The brimstone corps uses @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.USyDPcSdzcm024zT]{Change Formation}. A brimstone corps unit typically knows the marching column and wedge formations.

action->First-class Charge.name: First-class Charge
action->First-class Charge.description: The brimstone corps rushes forward with a hunger for battle. They Stride up to twice their Speed. At the end of their movement, each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] takes @Damage[(2d6+5)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage, with a @Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save.

Captivating Gem.name: Captivating Gem
Captivating Gem.descriptionHazard: The floating gem flares with inner radiance, sending out beams of light to captivate and drain the vitality from all who approach.
Captivating Gem.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:35] (expert), @Check[occultism|dc:35] (expert), or @Check[religion|dc:35] (expert) to unravel the magical matrix within the gem, @Check[crafting|dc:40] or @Check[thievery|dc:40] check to unbalance the crystal's alignment, or a @Check[athletics|dc:43] check to damage one of the gem's facets and change its geometry; two successes are required to disable the hazard
Captivating Gem.routine: (3 actions) The captivating gem attempts uses all three of its actions to incapacitate creatures. It targets the closest creature with its Freezing Light ability, then a different creature with its facet flare Strike. If a creature has already been @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} by Freezing Light, it chooses a different target for that ability.

action->Freezing Light.name: Freezing Light
action->Freezing Light.description:
The captivating gem shines intense light on a living creature, freezing it in place. The target must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] Save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} and takes @Damage[2d12[force]] damage.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} for 1 round and takes @Damage[4d12[force]] damage.

Critical Failure The target is paralyzed for 1 round and takes @Damage[8d12[force]] damage.

action->Glint.name: Glint
action->Glint.description:
Trigger Any living creature approaches within 10 feet of the gem

Effect The captivating gem targets the triggering creature with a facet flare Strike and then rolls initiative.

strike-melee->Facet Flare.name: Facet Flare
strike-melee->Facet Flare.description: The target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}

Cathilia's Meddling.name: Cathilia's Meddling
Cathilia's Meddling.descriptionHazard: A Hellknight on horseback shouts commands, beseeching all to return to order.
Cathilia's Meddling.disable: @Check[Hellknight Lore|dc:21] (trained) to convince the Hellknight that she's adding to chaos, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:25] to request she stop yelling
Cathilia's Meddling.routine: (3 actions) Cathilia uses her Hellknight's Orders Strike on creatures engaged in open fighting in area A2. She randomly chooses between targets in the likely case that there are multiple combatants.

action->Stop, Burglar.name: Stop, Burglar
action->Stop, Burglar.description:
Trigger A creature other than Zefiro opens the Museum's gates

Effect Cathilia shouts at the triggering creature. The target must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:27] save or have their action disrupted, and then Cathilia's meddling rolls initiative.

Champion of Asmodeus.name: Champion of Asmodeus
Champion of Asmodeus.publicNotes:
The deific champion is the paragon of champions, representing the truest form of devotion and dedication to a deity.

Religions inspire devout individuals to uphold their tenets.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

weapon->Longsword.name: Longsword
weapon->Longsword.description: Longswords, also known as arming swords, can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

shield->Steel Shield.name: Steel Shield
shield->Steel Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

HardnessHPBT
52010

armor->Full Plate.name: Full Plate
armor->Full Plate.description: Plate mail consists of interlocking plates that encase nearly the entire body in a carapace of steel. It is costly and heavy, and the wearer often requires help to don it correctly, but it provides some of the best defense armor can supply. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.zBYEU9E7034ENCmh]{Padded Armor} and a pair of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Ix2vicchE79d6Cl3]{gauntlets}.

equipment->Religious Symbol of Asmodeus.name: Religious Symbol of Asmodeus
equipment->Religious Symbol of Asmodeus.description: This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol can be worn on the body on a chain or pin, or can be held.

consumable->Potion of Flying (Standard).name: Potion of Flying (Standard)
consumable->Potion of Flying (Standard).description:
Activate A (manipulate)

Upon drinking this effervescent concoction, you gain a fly Speed of 40 feet for 1 minute.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.Mf9EBLhYmZerf0nS]{Effect: Potion of Flying (Standard)}

consumable->Viper's Fang.name: Viper's Fang
consumable->Viper's Fang.description:
Activate R (concentrate)

Trigger A creature within your reach uses a manipulate or move action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a moving action it's using

Requirements You are a master with the affixed weapon

When you activate this resin-strengthened viper skull, make a melee Strike against the triggering creature.

If you have Reactive Strike, you can activate the viper's fang as a free action.

If your attack is a critical hit and the trigger was a manipulate action, you disrupt that action.

This Strike doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.

If you hit with this attack, the creature is exposed to viper fang venom.

Viper Fang Venom (poison)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:32]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[3d10[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1}.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Blessed Shield.name: Blessed Shield
action->Blessed Shield.description: In the deific champion's hands, a shield gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.bvROhtzAez0KP7Vt]{moderate reinforcing rune}, giving it Hardness 8, 84 HP, and BT 42.

action->Deific Reactions.name: Deific Reactions
action->Deific Reactions.description: At the start of each of their turns, the deific champion gains an additional reaction they can only use to make a Reactive Strike or to Shield Block.

action->Champion's Aura.name: Champion's Aura
action->Champion's Aura.description:
15 feet.

Any follower of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.deities.Item.QRkcFciCOmFoxF1B]{Asmodeus} in the aura knows the champion is a champion of Asmodeus. Enemies in the champion's aura take a –1 circumstance penalty to saving throws against fear. In addition, an enemy that ends its turn in the champion's aura can't reduce the value of its frightened condition below 1.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.aVxksct3yToQUFAC]{Effect: Champion's Aura (Champion of Asmodeus)}

action->Champion's Courage.name: Champion's Courage
action->Champion's Courage.description: When the champion becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened}, they reduce the condition value by 1 (to a minimum of 0).

action->Exalted Retributive Strike.name: Exalted Retributive Strike
action->Exalted Retributive Strike.description:
Trigger An enemy damages the deific champion's ally, and both are in the deific champion's aura

Effect The ally gains resistance 14 to all damage against the triggering damage. If the enemy is within reach, the deific champion makes a melee Strike against it. Each ally in the champion's aura can spend a reaction to Strike the target with a –5 penalty.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.KzwcLwJ4c6hon0pu]{Effect: Exalted Retributive Strike}

action->Will Not Fall.name: Will Not Fall
action->Will Not Fall.description:
Trigger The deific champion's Hit Points are reduced to 0 for the first time that day

Effect The champion presses on, refusing to fail their god. They remain standing with 25 Hit Points.

action->Viper's Fang.name: Viper's Fang
action->Viper's Fang.description:
Trigger A creature within your reach uses a manipulate or move action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a moving action it's using

Requirements You are a master with the affixed weapon

When you activate this resin-strengthened viper skull, make a melee Strike against the triggering creature.

If you have Reactive Strike, you can activate the viper's fang as a free action.

If your attack is a critical hit and the trigger was a manipulate action, you disrupt that action.

This Strike doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.

If you hit with this attack, the creature is exposed to viper fang venom.

Viper Fang Venom (poison)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:32]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[3d10[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1}.

action->Will Not Falter.name: Will Not Falter
action->Will Not Falter.description:
Effect The deific champion declares their devotion to their deity and their cause. They Stride, then make a melee Strike. If the Strike hits an enemy, all allies within their champion's aura gain a +2 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws against fear until the start of the deific champion's next turn.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.N402OXwxrqVkcK87]{Effect: Will Not Falter}

Collapsing Egorian Building.name: Collapsing Egorian Building
Collapsing Egorian Building.descriptionHazard: Tremors from the changing landscape could cause a building to collapse.
Collapsing Egorian Building.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:45] (master), @Check[crafting|dc:45] (master), or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:45] (master) to brace the structure against collapse until the end of the creature's next turn. The DC of the flat check for this round (see Routine) decreases by 4 on a success (or by 8 on a critical success). Decreasing the flat check DC to 0 stabilizes the structure, ending this hazard.
Collapsing Egorian Building.reset: The building becomes susceptible to Shake to Pieces again [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} after it's stabilized.
Collapsing Egorian Building.routine: (1 action) The GM rolls a @Check[flat|dc:11], decreasing the DC as noted above. If the check fails, dust and debris fall within the building and 30 feet around it, providing concealment for 1 round and dealing @Damage[10d6[bludgeoning]] damage to 1d4 randomly chosen targets in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save); a target that critically fails this save is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the flat check critically fails, the effect is the same, but damage from the debris increases to 16d6, it affects 2d4 randomly chosen targets, and a creature that critically fails its save is knocked prone and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by the rubble ([[/act escape dc=40]], [[/act force-open dc=44]]).
Collapsing Egorian Building.stealth: (expert) to notice cracks forming in the walls of the structure

action->Shake to Pieces.name: Shake to Pieces
action->Shake to Pieces.description:
Trigger The Hellfire tornado moves adjacent to the building

Effect The building trembles. The floors of the building and the streets within 30 feet of the building become difficult terrain; creatures on this difficult terrain take a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, AC, and skill checks. The hazard rolls initiative.

Crowncrusher Bulwark.name: Crowncrusher Bulwark
Crowncrusher Bulwark.publicNotes: Stone bulwarks are slow and steady constructs typically carved from marble or granite. They're often made to serve as works of art when at rest, so some magical crafters employ master sculptors to ensure their constructs make beautiful statues. Older stone bulwarks might be weathered, with scuffed or cracked surfaces or missing noses and digits, but this weathering is largely cosmetic and doesn't adversely impact the bulwark's functionality.

action->Statuary Aura.name: Statuary Aura
action->Statuary Aura.description:
20 feet.

Rocks of marble magically arise from the ground in the aura. They protect the bulwark's allies, giving each of them standard @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.other-effects.Item.I9lfZUiCwMiGogVi]{Cover}. These stones can be used for Throw Rock.

This aura automatically activates at the start of the stone bulwark's first turn in combat and deactivates at the end of combat.

action->Binding Stone.name: Binding Stone
action->Binding Stone.description: Any creature hit by the stone bulwark's fist or rock Strike is affected by a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gPvtmKMRpg9I9D7H]{Earthbind} spell.

action->Inexorable March.name: Inexorable March
action->Inexorable March.description: The stone bulwark Strides up to its Speed, pushing back each creature whose space it moves into and damaging them if they try to stop its movement. A creature can attempt to bar the way by succeeding at a @Check[fortitude|dc:34] save. On a critical success, the resisting creature takes no damage; otherwise it is damaged as if hit by the construct's fist.

Crowncrusher Riot.name: Crowncrusher Riot
Crowncrusher Riot.descriptionHazard: A crew of anarchists rush to fight the dottari.
Crowncrusher Riot.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:21] to force them to back down, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:25] (expert) to request that they only injure the dottari
Crowncrusher Riot.routine: (2 actions) The rioters lob bricks and other heavy objects at any dottari excruciator divisions as an action, dealing @Damage[(2d6+10)[bludgeoning]] damage to the dottari and any adjacent creatures (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save).

action->While They're Distracted.name: While They're Distracted
action->While They're Distracted.description:
Trigger A dottari excruciator division deals damage to a living creature

Effect The Crowncrusher riot throws a volley of homemade explosives that deals @Damage[2d10[fire]] damage to the triggering creature and all creatures within 10 feet of it (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save), and then rolls initiative.

weapon->Trident.name: Trident
weapon->Trident.description: This three-pronged, spear-like weapon typically has a 4-foot shaft. Like a spear, it can be wielded with one hand or thrown.

action->Alleviation Vulnerability.name: Alleviation Vulnerability
action->Alleviation Vulnerability.description: Whenever an effect restores at least one foe within 30 feet of the cultist to the target's full Hit Points, the cultist takes @Damage[2d8[mental]] damage.

action->Amplify Torment.name: Amplify Torment
action->Amplify Torment.description: The cultist's Strikes deal an additional @Damage[1d8[mental]] damage to creatures that are injured. This additional damage increases to @Damage[1d12[mental]] if the target has fewer than half its HP remaining.

weapon->Mace.name: Mace
weapon->Mace.description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and allows its wielder to deliver powerful blows and dent armor.

shield->Sturdy Shield (Major).name: Sturdy Shield (Major)
shield->Sturdy Shield (Major).description:
With a superior design and excellent craftsmanship, this steel shield has higher Hardness than its non-magical counterparts, making it harder to break and destroy.

The shield has Hardness 17, HP 136, and BT 68.

armor->Hellknight Plate.name: Hellknight Plate
armor->Hellknight Plate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight half plate is the armor of choice for Hellknight signifiers, and Hellknight breastplate serves those in the order who lack the training to wear heavy armor.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

action->Rejuvenation.name: Rejuvenation
action->Rejuvenation.description:
When a graveknight is destroyed, their armor rebuilds their body over the course of [[/gmr 1d10]] days—or more quickly if the armor is worn by a living host. If the body is destroyed before then, the process restarts.

A graveknight can only be permanently destroyed by obliterating their armor (such as with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.r7ihOgKv19eJQnik]{Disintegrate}), transporting it to the Forge of Creation, or throwing it into the heart of a volcano.

action->Sacrilegious Aura.name: Sacrilegious Aura
action->Sacrilegious Aura.description:
30 feet.

When a creature in the aura uses a vitality spell or ability, the graveknight automatically attempts to counteract it, with a counteract modifier of [[/r 1d20+0 #Counteract]]{+34}.

action->Devastating Blast.name: Devastating Blast
action->Devastating Blast.description:
The graveknight unleashes a @Template[cone|distance:30] of energy. Creatures in the area take @Damage[21d6[mental]] with a @Check[will|dc:40|basic|options:area-effect] save.

The graveknight can use this ability once every [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Devastating Blast]] rounds.

action->Graveknight's Curse.name: Graveknight's Curse
action->Graveknight's Curse.description:
This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour

Saving Throw @Check[will|dc:42] save

Onset 1 hour

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} and can't remove armor (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 2}, –10-foot status penalty to Speeds, and can't remove armor (1 day)

Stage 3 dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight.

action->Phantom Mount.name: Phantom Mount
action->Phantom Mount.description:
HP 125; AC 40, Fort +32, Ref +27, Will +29

The graveknight summons a supernatural mount, as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.WPKJOhEihhcIm2uQ]{Marvelous Mount} heightened to a rank equal to half the graveknight's level. Unlike marvelous mount, the steed's AC and saving throw bonuses are all 4 lower than the graveknight's, and the steed has one-third the graveknight's Hit Points (rounded down).

If the steed is destroyed, the graveknight must wait 1 hour before using this ability again.

action->Ruinous Weapons.name: Ruinous Weapons
action->Ruinous Weapons.description: Any weapon the graveknight wields or unarmed attack the graveknight uses gains the effects of the greater fearsome weapon rune, deals an additional 1d6 mental damage, and functions as a +1 striking weapon rune.

action->Weapon Master.name: Weapon Master
action->Weapon Master.description: The graveknight has access to the critical specialization effects of any weapons it wields.

Deadly Spinning Blade Pillar.name: Deadly Spinning Blade Pillar
Deadly Spinning Blade Pillar.descriptionHazard: A spinning metal pole covered in deadly blades is hidden in the ceiling, connected to several trigger plates around the room and a hidden control panel built into the bed frame.
Deadly Spinning Blade Pillar.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:39] (master) three times on a pillar, or @Check[thievery|dc:37] (master) twice on the control panel deactivates the whole trap. Breaking the control panel prevents anyone from disabling the trap using the control panel and prevents the trap from deactivating automatically (see Reset below)
Deadly Spinning Blade Pillar.reset: The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
Deadly Spinning Blade Pillar.routine: (3 actions) The trap uses its first action to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature. Its second action is to move in a random direction (roll 1d4 to determine the direction), and its third action to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature.
Deadly Spinning Blade Pillar.stealth: (master) or DC 48 (master) to find the control panel

action->Descending Pillar.name: Descending Pillar
action->Descending Pillar.description:
Trigger A creature steps on one of the marked trigger plates

Effect The pillar descends from the ceiling at the marked grid intersection and makes a spinning blade Strike against one adjacent creature (if any). The trap then rolls initiative.

weapon->Mace.name: Mace
weapon->Mace.description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and allows its wielder to deliver powerful blows and dent armor.

armor->Explorer's Clothing.name: Explorer's Clothing
armor->Explorer's Clothing.description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex Cap and can grant an item bonus to AC if etched with potency runes.

action->Choking Aura.name: Choking Aura
action->Choking Aura.description: 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it takes @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:38|basic] save). A creature that fails this save is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 3} on a critical failure).

action->Mark of Fate.name: Mark of Fate
action->Mark of Fate.description: A creature that slays the deific vessel must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:41] save or be visibly marked as anathema to Asmodeus. It gains weakness 15 to unholy and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Charisma-based skill checks against followers of Asmodeus. Creatures attempting to [[/act track]] or [[/act gather-information]] about the creature gain a +2 circumstance bonus to their checks. The mark can't be hidden but can be removed by participating in an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.7Fd4lxozd11MQ55N]{Atone} ritual led by a worshipper of Asmodeus who's 15th level or higher.

action->Furious Flames.name: Furious Flames
action->Furious Flames.description: The vessel's unarmed attacks and any weapon they wield gain the greater flaming and greater grievous runes, and their Strikes gain the critical specialization effects of the appropriate weapon group.

Diabolic Vigor.name: Diabolic Vigor
Diabolic Vigor.descriptionHazard: Tendrils of infernal energy reach out from the Inferno Gate like tentacles, infusing the creatures that serve it, even tangentially, with vitality and protection from harm.
Diabolic Vigor.disable: @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to pray for divine intervention in counterbalancing this infernal influence; @Check[arcana|dc:35] (expert), @Check[nature|dc:35] (expert), or @Check[occultism|dc:35] (expert) to exert a magical force of will greater than the gate's influence; @Check[athletics|dc:38] (expert) or Thievery (expert) to disrupt the pentagram surrounding the Inferno Gate; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (7th rank; counteract DC 30) to cut off the magical energies. Magical checks to disable this hazard can be attempted from up to 30 feet away from the Inferno Gate, but physical checks require physically touching the pentagram; three successes are required to disrupt the diabolic vigor hazard.
Diabolic Vigor.routine: (1 action) On its turn, the diabolic vigor hazard empowers the merztorlegon and their bound armies within 200 feet of the Inferno Gate. It grants the siege devil regeneration 20 and reduces their weakness to holy from 20 to 10. The armies gain regeneration 10 and reduce their weakness to area damage and splash damage by 5. These effects last for 1 round.

action->Burst of Power.name: Burst of Power
action->Burst of Power.description:
Trigger Encounter E4 begins

Effect The Inferno Gate sends out beams of reddish energy to all devils and their bound armies within 200 feet, reducing all of their weaknesses by 10 for 1 round. The diabolic vigor hazard then rolls initiative.

armor->Explorer's Clothing.name: Explorer's Clothing
armor->Explorer's Clothing.description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex Cap and can grant an item bonus to AC if etched with potency runes.

equipment->Tailoring Toolkit (Sterling).name: Tailoring Toolkit (Sterling)
equipment->Tailoring Toolkit (Sterling).description:
You need these tools to create items from raw materials with the Craft skill. Sterling artisan's tools give you a +1 item bonus to the check.

Different sets are needed for different work, as determined by the GM; for example, a blacksmith's toolkit differs from a woodworker's toolkit. If you wear your artisan's toolkit, you can draw and replace it as part of the action that uses it.

action->Sartorial Snag.name: Sartorial Snag
action->Sartorial Snag.description:
Trigger An adjacent creature uses a manipulate action or a move action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using

Effect The disciple drives several threaded needles through the triggering creature's clothing and pulls, momentarily binding its outfit and restricting movement. The disciple attempts a @Check[Tailoring Lore] check against the triggering creature's AC. If the triggering creature isn't wearing at least a cloak, sash, tabard, or similar amount of visible cloth, apply a –2 circumstance penalty to this check.

Critical Success As success, plus the disciple disrupts the action.

Success Threads partly entangle the triggering creature, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to it Speeds until the beginning of its next turn. Any creature within reach of the targeted creature can end these effects with an Interact action.

Failure The triggering creature is unaffected.

Critical Failure The disciple becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the beginning of their next turn.

action->Pins and Needles.name: Pins and Needles
action->Pins and Needles.description: The disciple gains access to the critical specialization effects of their sleeve and needle Strikes, with a DC of 34.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The disciple's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Tailor-Made Armaments.name: Tailor-Made Armaments
action->Tailor-Made Armaments.description: While wearing clothing worth at least 10 gp, a disciple of thread can cause their sleeves or other parts of their outfit to extend and animate as unarmed Strikes in the flail weapon group. While wielding these sleeves, the cultist can use their Tailoring Lore modifier in place of Athletics to [[/act grapple skill=tailoring-lore]], [[/act reposition skill=tailoring-lore]], and [[/act trip skill=tailoring-lore]]. As a free action while carrying a tailoring toolkit, the disciple can Draw mundane sewing needles to use as lethal throwing weapons in the bow weapon group.

action->Divine Devastation.name: Divine Devastation
action->Divine Devastation.description: Each time the divine warden army loses a segment and when they're reduced to 0 Hit Points, divine energy erupts from the fallen, dealing @Damage[8d6[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage to each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] (@Check[will|dc:38|basic] save). On a failure, a creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 day (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure).

action->Divine Lance Fusillade.name: Divine Lance Fusillade
action->Divine Lance Fusillade.description: The divine warden army unleashes beams of divine energy. This fusillade is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet that deals @Damage[9d6[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:37|basic|traits:holy,magical] save. When the divine warden army is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Holy Blades.name: Holy Blades
action->Holy Blades.description:
The divine wardens make coordinated melee attacks with their swords. Each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:37|basic] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(2d8+1)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d8+12)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(6d8+13)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Orchestra of Faith.name: Orchestra of Faith
action->Orchestra of Faith.description: The divine warden army is a beacon for the faithful. A cleric of Iomedae can channel a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]{Heal} spell through a divine warden they can see within 60 feet. The cleric determines any targets or area for the spell as if they were standing in a space of the divine warden army of their choosing.

Dottari Excruciator Division.name: Dottari Excruciator Division
Dottari Excruciator Division.publicNotes:
The dottari serve as Cheliax's elite city guards, assembled and deployed to quash serious threats. Queen Abrogail Thrune I founded the original excruciator divisions to restore order to a nation torn by civil war and not yet fully submissive to Thrune rule. To this day, the dottari are empowered to destroy property (or break a few bones) in carrying out their duties.

Dottari are trained to neutralize foes nonlethally, but they empowered to use lethal force at their discretion (and often do). They're also famed for their alchemical crossbow bolts—many tipped with fast-acting, explosive glue—ending altercations before they've really begun.

action->Long Arm of the Law.name: Long Arm of the Law
action->Long Arm of the Law.description:
Trigger A creature adjacent to the dottari excruciator division leaves a square during a move action it's using

Effect The excruciators deal @Damage[(2d8+9)[bludgeoning]] damage to the triggering creature with a @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save. On a critical failure, the creature's move action is disrupted.

action->Bash It Down.name: Bash It Down
action->Bash It Down.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The excruciators make a coordinated effort to shove outward with their shields, taking down opponents and impediments alike. The troop attempts to either [[/act shove]] or [[/act trip]] each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] and [[/act force-open]] each object in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5]. The troop rolls a single Athletics check and compares the result to each target's appropriate DC. The troop doesn't take a penalty for not having a crowbar. It's possible for the troop to get a different degree of success for each target. The troop can't automatically Stride after any creature they successfully Shove.

action->Beat the Lawbreakers.name: Beat the Lawbreakers
action->Beat the Lawbreakers.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The excruciators swing their maces in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). The damage depends on the number of actions. The excruciators can choose to deal nonlethal damage with this attack.

1 @Damage[(1d6+2)[bludgeoning]]

2 @Damage[(2d6+11)[bludgeoning]]

3 @Damage[(3d6+14)[bludgeoning]]

action->Stop Where You Are.name: Stop Where You Are
action->Stop Where You Are.description: The excruciators draw or reload their crossbows with special hindering bolts, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 80 feet that deals @Damage[4d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). When the dottari excruciator division is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5]. On a failure, a creature also takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. On a critical failure, a creature also gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} condition ([[/act escape dc=25]]).

Dream Drowner.name: Dream Drowner
Dream Drowner.publicNotes: Dream drowners are anemone-like predators from Orv's flooded vaults. A dream drowner indefinitely parasitizes and mentally controls a host to serve as its mount as it seeks out the thoughts of thinking prey to consume. So far, any attempt to communicate with these creatures elicits aggression, yet the beings' behavior demonstrates extraordinary intelligence.
Dream Drowner.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Entrancing Movement.name: Entrancing Movement
action->Entrancing Movement.description:
30 feet.

The dream drowner's tentacles move with hypnotic grace. When a creature ends its turn in the dream drowner's aura, it must attempt a @Check[will|dc:32] save, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} on a failure (or increasing its stupefied value by 1, to a maximum of 4). The dream drowner can deactivate or activate this aura by using a single action, which has the concentrate trait.

action->Eldritch Toxin.name: Eldritch Toxin
action->Eldritch Toxin.description: A creature touched by a dream drowner's tentacles must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:29] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} on a critical failure).

action->Entangled Thoughts.name: Entangled Thoughts
action->Entangled Thoughts.description: A creature affected by a dream drowner's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.OsOhx3TGIZ7AhD0P]{Dominate} spell becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 minute after the dominate spell ends. The creature can attempt a @Check[will|dc:32] save at the end of each of its turns to end the condition.

action->Mind Leech.name: Mind Leech
action->Mind Leech.description: When a creature fails or critically fails a Will save against a dream drowner's occult innate spells, the creature takes @Damage[1d6[mental]|traits:nonlethal]{1d6 nonlethal mental} damage per rank of the spell.

action->Subjugate Minds.name: Subjugate Minds
action->Subjugate Minds.description:
A dream drowner assaults the minds of distracted creatures. Each creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] that has the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} or stupefied condition takes @Damage[10d8[mental]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[will|dc:32|basic] save).

The dream drowner can't use this ability again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge this ability]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Tentacle Flurry.name: Tentacle Flurry
action->Tentacle Flurry.description: The dream drowner makes a tentacle Strike against each creature within its reach. These Strikes count toward the dream drowner's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the dream drowner makes all its attacks.

Elder Sweet Hag.name: Elder Sweet Hag
Elder Sweet Hag.publicNotes:
Sweet hags use bright colors, pleasant visages, and sugary treats to draw in children and the young at heart. Their favorite prey is impoverished youngsters who are unlikely to be missed—sweet hags offer them food and a veneer of kindness before devouring the hapless child. Sweet hags also enchant their food to magically charm others, often using these bespelled servitors to handle all of their busywork. Though they present a lovely and generous face to the world, they turn vicious when insulted or defied. A sweet hag's true form is a dribbling mass of flesh with gumdrop eyes, often resembling melting taffy.

Hags are malevolent predators who use magic and manipulation to lure children and young adults into their clutches. Though their true forms are eldritch and horrifying, hags spend much of their lives disguised as ordinary women. They seek out targets who are unhappy, innocent, or otherwise vulnerable, preying on their weaknesses before snatching them up. The typical hag is abusive, controlling, and narcissistic. Though less malicious hags possibly exist, they rarely reveal their true forms, making them nearly impossible to find.

action->Coven.name: Coven
action->Coven.description:
A sweet hag adds @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.vLA0q0WOK2YPuJs6]{Charm}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.JyxTmqjYYn63V5LY]{Honeyed Words}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.KSAEhNfZyXMO7Z7V]{Outcast's Curse} to their coven's spells.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Coven]

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Betraying Touch.name: Betraying Touch
action->Betraying Touch.description: The sweet hag touches a creature that doesn't realize the hag is an enemy. The betrayed creature is affected by soporific strike with a –4 circumstance penalty to their saving throw.

action->Change Shape.name: Change Shape
action->Change Shape.description:
The sweet hag can take on the appearance of any Medium humanoid woman. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

action->Poisoned Candy.name: Poisoned Candy
action->Poisoned Candy.description: The sweet hag casts an innate spell that can normally target 1 creature on a piece of food, typically a sweet treat. The spell is stored in the food. The first creature that eats any of the food is affected by the spell and takes a –4 circumstance penalty to their saving throw against that effect.

action->Soporific Touch.name: Soporific Touch
action->Soporific Touch.description:
A creature damaged by a sweet hag's claw must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 day.

If the creature critically fails or fails this save while already enfeebled by soporific strike, it falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} and dreams of eating delicious sweets; this is a mental sleep effect. If not woken up before 1 minute passes, the creature wakes up automatically.

action->Thrusting Flurry.name: Thrusting Flurry
action->Thrusting Flurry.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The waveriders launch coordinated attacks against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] with a @Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] save. The damage depends on the number of actions. 1 @Damage[(1d8+6)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage 2 @Damage[(3d8+6)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage 3 @Damage[(4d8+9)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Wavecrash.name: Wavecrash
action->Wavecrash.description: The waveriders conjure a cresting wave beneath their feet and use it ride down their enemies. The waveriders Stride up to double their Speed and can move through the spaces of Medium or smaller creatures or troops composed of Medium or smaller creatures. Creatures in the path of the waveriders take @Damage[(1d8+6)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save).

Feedback Flow.name: Feedback Flow
Feedback Flow.descriptionHazard: The fetid material in this pit sloshes around in a disgusting feedback loop, filled with large pieces of solid trash that risk bludgeoning creatures inside it to death!
Feedback Flow.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:27] or @Check[thievery|dc:27] (trained) to move without causing a wave of filth, or @Check[athletics|dc:30] or @Check[crafting|dc:30] (trained) to stabilize the trash heap and prevent it from flowing; three successes are required to fully stabilize and disable the hazard.
Feedback Flow.routine:
(1 to 3 actions) The feedback flow hazard uses up to 3 actions to make flowing trash Strikes against living creatures that take move actions inside the refuse pit (area E7) but can only target an individual creature once per round. The ofalths, used to living in this pit, don't trigger these attacks. Additionally, any non-ofalth creature that ends its turn inside the refuse pit must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} until the end of its next turn, then is immune to this effect for 1 minute.

Failure The creature is sickened 1.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

action->Wobbling Waves.name: Wobbling Waves
action->Wobbling Waves.description:
Trigger Any Medium or larger non-ofalth creature takes a move action inside area E7

Effect The feedback flow makes a flowing trash Strike against the triggering creature and then rolls initiative.

Fiendish Mantis.name: Fiendish Mantis
Fiendish Mantis.publicNotes:
These gigantic mantids make their homes within prehistoric forests.

These predators possess lightning-quick forelegs and a bone-breaking bite.

action->Ship-Bound.name: Ship-Bound
action->Ship-Bound.description: The mantis is fused to the ship and moves where the ship moves. It doesn't occupy a space of its own but is treated as being in the front 20-foot-by-20-foot space of the ship for the purpose of making attacks and being targeted.

action->Fling.name: Fling
action->Fling.description: The deadly mantis flings a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} creature into the air, up to 30 feet overhead and up to 30 feet away from the mantis (the creature takes damage from the fall as normal). If the flung creature lands on another creature, the creature it lands on takes the same amount of bludgeoning damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:31|basic] save.

action->Rending Mandibles.name: Rending Mandibles
action->Rending Mandibles.description: The mantis makes a mandibles Strike against a creature it has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}. If that Strike hits and the creature is wearing armor with Hardness 12 or lower, the armor is broken. This Strike doesn't further damage armor that's already broken.

action->Sudden Strike.name: Sudden Strike
action->Sudden Strike.description: On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the deadly mantis.

First-Class Cavalry.name: First-Class Cavalry
First-Class Cavalry.publicNotes: Soldiers who participate in multiple campaigns of war gain the experience necessary to become their military's finest fighting forces. first-class cavalry troops are deployed on battlefields where winning the day is of the utmost importance.

action->No Retreat.name: No Retreat
action->No Retreat.description: These soldiers have been extensively trained to hold their ground no matter the situation. If any effect would force the first-class cavalry to move, reduce the distance by 10 feet. Any time they would be affected by the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition, the first-class cavalry is instead @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for the same duration.

action->Bolt Salvo.name: Bolt Salvo
action->Bolt Salvo.description: The first-class cavalry draws, loads, and shoots a salvo from their crossbows. The salvo is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 120 feet that deals @Damage[4d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save). When the first-class cavalry is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Clash of Steel.name: Clash of Steel
action->Clash of Steel.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The first-class cavalry attacks each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(2d6+1)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d6+10)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(4d6+18)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Drilled in Formations.name: Drilled in Formations
action->Drilled in Formations.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The first-class cavalry uses @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.USyDPcSdzcm024zT]{Change Formation}. An first-class cavalry unit typically knows the marching column and wedge formations.

action->First-class Charge.name: First-class Charge
action->First-class Charge.description: The first-class cavalry rushes forward with a hunger for battle. They Stride up to twice their Speed. At the end of their movement, each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] takes @Damage[(2d6+5)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage, with a @Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save.

First-Class Vordines.name: First-Class Vordines
First-Class Vordines.publicNotes: Soldiers who participate in multiple campaigns of war gain the experience necessary to become their military's finest fighting forces. First-class vordines troops are deployed on battlefields where winning the day is of the utmost importance.

action->No Retreat.name: No Retreat
action->No Retreat.description: These soldiers have been extensively trained to hold their ground no matter the situation. If any effect would force the first-class vordines to move, reduce the distance by 10 feet. Any time they would be affected by the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition, the first-class vordines is instead @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for the same duration.

action->Bolt Salvo.name: Bolt Salvo
action->Bolt Salvo.description: The first-class vordines draw, load, and shoot a salvo from their crossbows. The salvo is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 120 feet that deals @Damage[4d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save). When the first-class vordines are reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Clash of Steel.name: Clash of Steel
action->Clash of Steel.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The first-class vordines attack each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(2d6+1)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d6+10)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(4d6+18)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Drilled in Formations.name: Drilled in Formations
action->Drilled in Formations.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The first-class vordines uses @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.USyDPcSdzcm024zT]{Change Formation}. An first-class vordines unit typically knows the marching column and wedge formations.

action->First-class Charge.name: First-class Charge
action->First-class Charge.description: The first-class vordines rushes forward with a hunger for battle. They Stride up to twice their Speed. At the end of their movement, each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] takes @Damage[(2d6+5)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage, with a @Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save.

Floor-Be-Gone.name: Floor-Be-Gone
Floor-Be-Gone.descriptionHazard: Hinged boards prop the massive paving stones into place on the causeway.
Floor-Be-Gone.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:27] (expert) to reinforce the stones of this causeway or @Check[thievery|dc:29] (master) to jam the hinges on the boards

action->Oops, There It Goes!.name: Oops, There It Goes!
action->Oops, There It Goes!.description:
Trigger A creature moves through a marked square and fails a secret @Check[flat|dc:5]

Effect The board's hinges release, causing the paving stones to drop—along with any creatures in the marked area—to the ground below. Affected creatures can attempt to [[/act grab-an-edge]] (DC 29). Creatures that fall take @Damage[(4d12+26)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic] save) as well as the typical falling damage.

Fog Hound.name: Fog Hound
Fog Hound.publicNotes:
Believed to be bred by the Kings of Hell themselves within vast kennels, greater hell hounds are the favored guards and hunting hounds of powerful fiends and, rarely, mortals who worship them and have earned their favor.

Hell hounds are fiendish, extraplanar canines hailing from the Pit that can hunt down quarry and breathe supernatural gouts of flame. They are temperamental and quick to exhibit aggressive behavior.

action->Hellish Revenge.name: Hellish Revenge
action->Hellish Revenge.description:
Trigger The greater hell hound is critically hit by any Strike
