Effect The greater hell hound's Hellfire Breath recharges. They can immediately use it as part of this reaction.

action->Fog Born.name: Fog Born
action->Fog Born.description: The fog hound's breathe fog and are composed of mist. Their jaws Strike and Hellfire Breath deal cold damage instead of fire damage and have the cold trait.

action->Hellfire Breath.name: Hellfire Breath
action->Hellfire Breath.description:
The hell hound breathes flames that deal @Damage[10d6[cold]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[cone|distance:15] (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic|options:area-effect] save.)

The hell hound can't use Hellfire Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Hellfire Breath]]{1d4 rounds}. If the greater hell hound would take cold damage or be targeted by a cold effect, their Hellfire Breath recharges.

action->Pack Attack.name: Pack Attack
action->Pack Attack.description: The greater hell hound's Strikes deal 1d8 extra damage to creatures within the reach of at least two of their allies.

Force and Flame.name: Force and Flame
Force and Flame.descriptionHazard: Four invisible runes on a large crate conjure jets of flame and whips of force when someone tries to open the container.
Force and Flame.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:45] (master) to erase each rune, @Check[arcana|dc:48] (master) to channel energy from one rune into another, destroying both, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (8th rank; counteract DC 45) to counteract each rune; a PC can choose to disable a fire rune or a force rune (or choose randomly)
Force and Flame.reset: The runes deactivate after 1 minute, but if at least one rune remains, the trap reactivates 1 round after that.
Force and Flame.routine: (4 actions) The trap has 1 fewer action for each rune that's been disabled or destroyed; whether that rune was a fire rune or a force rune affects the actions the trap can take. On the trap's initiative, the two fire runes each blast @Template[type:cone|distance:30]s of flame as an action in opposite directions that deal @Damage[12d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:44|basic] save). Then the two force runes each make a force whip Strike as an action against different targets.

action->Blinding Alarm.name: Blinding Alarm
action->Blinding Alarm.description:
Trigger A creature tries to open the crate without removing the runes

Effect The four runes become active with a flash of red light. Each creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:42] save. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is slightly dazed by the flash, taking a –2 circumstance penalty to their initiative.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to their initiative.

Critical Failure The target is blinded for 1 minute and takes a –4 circumstance penalty to their initiative.

weapon->Spear.name: Spear
weapon->Spear.description: A long metal shaft ending with a metal spike, a spear can be used one-handed as a melee weapon and can be thrown.

shield->Corundum Shield.name: Corundum Shield
shield->Corundum Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

HardnessHPBT
52010

action->Heavy Wingbeats.name: Heavy Wingbeats
action->Heavy Wingbeats.description: The Glittercrag strix uses their powerful muscles to gain altitude through raw power. They can Step twice and Strike once in any order. Instead of Stepping, the strix can instead Fly up to 10 feet, ignoring difficult terrain caused by flying upward.

weapon->Handwraps of Mighty Blows.name: Handwraps of Mighty Blows
weapon->Handwraps of Mighty Blows.description:
As you invest these embroidered strips of cloth, you must meditate and slowly wrap them around your hands. These handwraps have weapon runes etched into them to give your unarmed attacks the benefits of those runes, making your unarmed attacks work like magic weapons. For example, +1 striking handwraps of mighty blows would give you a +1 item bonus to attack rolls with your unarmed attacks and increase the damage of your unarmed attacks from one weapon die to two (normally 2d4 instead of 1d4, but if your fists have a different weapon damage die or you have other unarmed attacks, use two of that die size instead).

You can upgrade, add, and transfer runes to and from the handwraps just as you would for a weapon, and you can attach talismans to the handwraps. Treat the handwraps as melee weapons of the brawling group with light Bulk for these purposes. Property runes apply only when they would be applicable to the unarmed attack you're using. For example, a property that must be applied to a slashing weapon wouldn't function when you attacked with a fist, but you would gain its benefits if you attacked with a claw or some other slashing unarmed attack.

weapon->Khakkhara.name: Khakkhara

Gorthoklek.name: Gorthoklek
Gorthoklek.publicNotes:
When an army of devils invades to bathe a region in bloodshed and hellfire, it is likely that one of the nessaris masterminded the incursion. Cunning, powerful, and ruthless, nessaris often serve the archdevils directly. They rule infernal duchies, subjugate mortal worlds, and usurp infernal rivals using unparalleled despotism and calculated ferocity. To realize their tyrannical machinations, nessaris claim mortal souls that they corrupt into ort servants, which can then be shaped and transformed through infernal manipulation into the terrifying devils that form Hell's formidable legions. They often select the most wicked and vicious orts for their armies, drawing upon these lesser devils' depravity during powerful magical ceremonies to create hideous and terrifying abominations that can cow and eviscerate the nessari's enemies.

Each nessari is crafted deep within the nightmarish bowels of Nessus, the ninth layer of Hell, to serve the whims of archdevils and infernal dukes. Those nessaris that don't leave Nessus to command infernal legions in the upper layers of Hell often form the courts of Hell's elite, gathering cronies and sects that shape Hell's political landscape through subterfuge and manipulation. But many nessaris see themselves as living embodiments of hellfire, the all-encompassing wrath of Hell, and thus prefer to dwell in realms consumed by fire. In Avernus, Dis, Malebolge, Nessus, and Phlegethon, nessaris build vast citadels of brimstone wreathed in flame to lord over.

Rather than being above the constant power struggles of the diabolic ranks, nessaris are in the thick of it. Even apocalyptic schemes to conquer or despoil mortal worlds usually begin from a desire to claw a single rank up the immortal hierarchy of Hell. For this reason, the best, yet also most dangerous, ally against a nessari is the nessari next to them.

Nessaris tower over other devils, standing at least 16 feet tall, weighing over 1,000 pounds, and brandishing wingspans in excess of 20 feet.

Infernal Dukes

Elite members of Hell's political infrastructure and leadership hierarchy, the dukes of Hell are chosen from among the most tyrannical, oppressive, and conniving devils. While not all infernal dukes are nessaris, a nessari's natural disposition toward conquest and oppression often makes them an ideal candidate for this position. To create an infernal duke, adjust the nessari to be between 21st and 25th level. Because of their physical prowess, manipulative nature, and powerful spellcasting abilities, infernal dukes make excellent villains for long-running campaigns.

Masters of corruption and architects of conquest, devils seek both to tempt mortal life to join in their pursuit of all things profane and to spread tyranny throughout all worlds. The temptations they offer mortals range from great powers granted by the signing of an infernal contract to twisted favors following a whispered pledge to a diabolic patron, or any number of even subtler exchanges. Those who succumb to these temptations find themselves consigned to an afterlife of endless torment in the pits of Hell, wherein the only hope of escape lies in the chance of being promoted to become a devil in the infernal ranks.

Every devil has a specific role to play in the upkeep of the remorseless bureaucratic machine that is Hell, from soldiers and scholars to inquisitors, lawyers, judges, and executioners. Lowly orts perform subservient labor to more powerful and specialized devils, such as infantry and contract devils, while the greatest nessaris command entire infernal armies.

Asmodeus stands at the apex of the structure he created, but the layers below him are marked by a constant jockeying for position. Most diabolic plans ultimately serve to improve the schemer's place in the hierarchy.

Greater Hellcat.name: Greater Hellcat
Greater Hellcat.publicNotes:
Hellcats are devious predators native to the fiery pits of Hell. While the fiendish creatures resemble undead skeletal smilodons, bones smoking with heat and dripping with boiling blood, they're actually living hellspawn whose transparent flesh reveals their burning skeleton. A typical hellcat is 9 feet long and weighs 1,000 pounds.

Left to their own devices, hellcats spend their time hunting. As fiendish creatures, they don't need mortal sustenance but devour prey for the sheer pleasure of inflicting pain. They're also far more intelligent than most assume, and they resent being treated as unintelligent animals; those who treat a hellcat thusly might find themselves made into a trophy for its pack, as a hellcat will go to great lengths to coordinate elaborate revenge upon any who fail to show them proper respect.

Though they can't speak, hellcats know Diabolic and can communicate by telepathy with any creature capable of speech. They rarely say much except to whisper threats and acknowledge the orders of their diabolic masters.

Hellcats are quick to retreat if clearly outmatched or up against foes they're unable to reach, but they never forget prey that escapes them.

effect->Effect: Hellfire Hydra Heads.description: The number of heads the hellfire hydra currently has.

action->Hellfire Regeneration.name: Hellfire Regeneration
action->Hellfire Regeneration.description:
The hellfire hydra has regeneration equal to 5 × the number of heads it has. If a hellfire hydra's body is missing any heads and the remaining stumps haven't been snuffed out, the hydra attempts a @Check[fortitude|dc:42] save after it regains Hit Points from regeneration. On a success, one non-snuffed stump regrows two heads; on a critical success, two nonsnuffed stumps regrow into two heads each. The hellfire hydra can never grow more than 10 heads. The hellfire hydra's regeneration only fully deactivates if all its heads are destroyed and all stumps are snuffed out, at which point it dies.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Regeneration]

action->Head Regrowth.name: Head Regrowth
action->Head Regrowth.description:
A hellfire hydra has five heads. A creature can attempt to sever one of the hydra's heads by specifically targeting it and dealing damage equal to the head's Hit Points. A head that isn't completely severed returns to full Hit Points at the end of any creature's turn.

A hellfire hydra can regrow a severed head using hellfire regeneration. A creature can prevent this regrowth by dealing damage to the stump with a cold or water effect, snuffing out its flames. Single-target cold or water effects need to be targeted at a specific stump, but effects that deal splash damage or affect areas covering the hellfire hydra's whole space snuff out all stumps if they deal damage and have the cold or water trait. If the attack that destroys a head deals any damage with the cold or water trait, the stump is snuffed out instantly. If all five heads are snuffed out, the hydra dies.

Hydra Head

  • Hit Points 50

  • Immunities Area Damage

  • Weaknesses Cold 15, Holy 10, Water 10

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.QigGoOYUG7BHuvE4]{Effect: Hellfire Hydra Heads}

action->Reactive Heads.name: Reactive Heads
action->Reactive Heads.description: A hellfire hydra gains an extra reaction per round for each of its heads beyond the first, which it can use only to make Reactive Strikes. It can't use more than 1 reaction on the same triggering action, even if a creature leaves several squares within its reach, and the hydra must use a different head for each Reactive Strike it makes. Whenever one of the hellfire hydra's heads is destroyed, the hydra loses 1 of its extra reactions per round.

action->Conflagration of Jaws.name: Conflagration of Jaws
action->Conflagration of Jaws.description: The hellfire hydra slams each of its heads into the ground, where they explode before quickly reforming. The hellfire hydra creates a number of @Template[type:burst|distance:5]s within 10 feet equal to its current number of heads. These bursts can't overlap. Each creature in a burst takes @Damage[6d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic] save). This counts as a number of attacks equal to the number of heads the hellfire hydra has toward the hydra's multiple attack penalty.

Hellfire Tornado.name: Hellfire Tornado
Hellfire Tornado.descriptionHazard: The very air catches fire from the heat of a dragon's breath, causing a burning tornado.
Hellfire Tornado.disable: The Hellfire tornado can't be disabled—only endured. It ends after 5 rounds.
Hellfire Tornado.reset: Once 5 rounds pass, the Hellfire tornado dissipates and doesn't reset.
Hellfire Tornado.routine:
(1 action; air, fire) The Hellfire tornado—a 15-footdiameter column 80 feet tall—moves 60 feet down the street. It can move through creatures' spaces, and creatures can move into its space, though the tornado's space is difficult terrain. Each creature the tornado moves through or each creature that ends its turn in the tornado's area takes @Damage[(4d10)[bludgeoning],(4d6)[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:40|basic|traits:air,fire] save). A creature can be affected by the tornado only once per round.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and rises 10 feet into the air as it's engulfed by the tornado ([[/act escape dc=40]]). While engulfed, a creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}, and if it doesn't have a fly Speed, it gains a 10-foot fly Speed until it either reaches the ground or ceases to be in the tornado's area, potentially falling when the hazard ends or when it leaves the tornado's area. A creature engulfed by the tornado moves along with it on the tornado's turn.

Critical Failure As failure, except the creature takes double damage and rises 20 feet into the air.

40 feet.

Hellknight Dragoon Squad.name: Hellknight Dragoon Squad
Hellknight Dragoon Squad.publicNotes: Some fugitives are so dangerous or numerous that they warrant a whole Hellknight squadron who can ride after quarry and dismount to give chase in crowded settlements. These Hellknights wield flails as capably as they wield fear, using both to subdue and restrain foes.
Hellknight Dragoon Squad.allSavesBonus: +2 circumstance bonus to saves vs fear effects

action->+2 Circumstance Bonus to Saves v.name: +2 Circumstance Bonus to Saves vs Fear Effects

action->Bind Them in Chains.name: Bind Them in Chains
action->Bind Them in Chains.description:
Trigger A creature or troop adjacent to the dragoon squad gains the frightened condition, or an adjacent troop is reduced to 2 segments

Effect Sensing their foes' faltering morale, the dragoons lash out with their manacles. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} until the end of the squad's next turn.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} until the end of the squad's next turn and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} until it [[/act escape dc=25]]{Escapes}.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} instead of immobilized.

action->Break the Weak Link.name: Break the Weak Link
action->Break the Weak Link.description:
10 feet.

As long as the dragoon squad has at least 3 segments, all enemy troops within the aura take a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against emotion effects.

action->Arrow Volley.name: Arrow Volley
action->Arrow Volley.description: The dragoons draw bows and fire arrows as a ranged attack. This volley is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 80 feet that deals @Damage[4d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). When the troop is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Fear the Chain.name: Fear the Chain
action->Fear the Chain.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The dragoons attempt to [[/act demoralize]] all foes in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:15], comparing the result of a single Intimidation check to each target's Will DC. The squad doesn't take the penalty if they don't speak the same language as a target. It's possible for the dragoon squad to get a different degree of success for each target, and a target is temporarily immune to this ability only if the squad critically fails the check against them.

action->Flail Assault.name: Flail Assault
action->Flail Assault.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The dragoons lash out with their flails in a melee attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d8+2)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage]

2 @Damage[(2d8+9)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage]

3 @Damage[(3d8+11)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage]

Hellknight Hunter Squad.name: Hellknight Hunter Squad
Hellknight Hunter Squad.publicNotes: Against immense prey, Order of the Pike Hellknights assemble elite squads trained to strike as one, shield each other from fiery breath, protect civilians, and stand strong against foes that would terrify lesser troops.

action->Hunters of Monsters.name: Hunters of Monsters
action->Hunters of Monsters.description: Hellknight hunters can use Monster Lore to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about creatures of any type. Whenever one of the squad's damaging abilities would affect only a single creature that lacks the swarm or troop trait, they gain a +2 status bonus to that ability's Reflex save DC.

action->Pin It Down.name: Pin It Down
action->Pin It Down.description:
Trigger An adjacent enemy uses a manipulate or move action, or leaves an adjacent space during a move action it's using

Effect The hunters stab with their longspears. The triggering enemy takes @Damage[(4d8+9)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic] save). If the enemy critically fails this saving throw, the triggering action is disrupted.

action->Disciplined Barricade.name: Disciplined Barricade
action->Disciplined Barricade.description: The Hellknights defensively lock their shields. Until the start of this troop's next turn, the troop and all allies within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] gain a +1 circumstance bonus to their AC and Reflex saving throws.

action->Hurl Javelins.name: Hurl Javelins
action->Hurl Javelins.description: The squad's members throw a volley of javelins. Each creature in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 30 feet of the troop takes @Damage[(5d6+9)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:33|basic] save. When the Hellknight hunters are reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Medic!.name: Medic!
action->Medic!.description: The hunters bandage an adjacent creature. The target regains @Damage[(5d8+20)[healing]] Hit Points, after which the target is immune to this ability for 1 day.

action->Phalanx Strike.name: Phalanx Strike
action->Phalanx Strike.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The Hellknight hunters strike outward with their longspears in a uniform melee attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic] save). The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d8+5)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d8+9)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(5d8+13)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

weapon->Mace.name: Mace
weapon->Mace.description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and allows its wielder to deliver powerful blows and dent armor.

shield->Steel Shield.name: Steel Shield
shield->Steel Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

HardnessHPBT
52010

armor->Hellknight Plate.name: Hellknight Plate
armor->Hellknight Plate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight half plate is the armor of choice for Hellknight signifiers, and Hellknight breastplate serves those in the order who lack the training to wear heavy armor.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

action->Champion's Aura.name: Champion's Aura
action->Champion's Aura.description: You're surrounded by an aura in a 15-foot emanation. It has the aura and divine traits. Any follower of your deity within the aura immediately knows you're a champion of your deity. This aura is used as the range for your champion's reaction and for various other effects. You can suppress or resume the aura as a single action, which has the concentrate trait, and it ends if you fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}.

action->Exalted Iron Command.name: Exalted Iron Command
action->Exalted Iron Command.description:
Trigger An enemy in your champion's aura damages you

Effect You put an impertinent foe who dared harm you in their proper place. You command each enemy in your champion's aura to kneel before you in obedience. If they dare to refuse, they must pay the price in pain and anguish. The enemy must choose one of the following options.

  • Kneel The enemy drops @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} as a free action.

  • Refuse You deal @Damage[(5d6)[mental],(5)[persistent,mental]] damage to the enemy.

Regardless of which option the enemy chose, your Strikes against it deal 3 extra spirit damage until the end of your next turn.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.2a6fuugiPpICe3Li]{Effect: Champion's Extra Damage}

action->Defensive Advance.name: Defensive Advance
action->Defensive Advance.description:
With the protection of your shield, you dive into battle! You Raise your Shield and Stride. If you end your movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, you can make a melee Strike against that enemy.

You can use Defensive Advance while Burrowing, Climbing, Flying, or Swimming instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type.

action->Gruesome Strike.name: Gruesome Strike
action->Gruesome Strike.description:
Requirements Your Strikes currently deal extra damage from your champion's reaction.

Make a Strike against the creature that triggered your champion's reaction. If you hit, double the extra damage the target takes from your reaction, and the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}. Regardless of the result, the creature is temporarily immune to your Gruesome Strike for 24 hours.

Hellknight Sea Brigade.name: Hellknight Sea Brigade
Hellknight Sea Brigade.publicNotes:
A Hellknight cavalry brigade consists of several Hellknights and a single field-maralictor, all wearing the distinctive armor of their order and wielding lances. The maralictor speaks for the brigade, questioning travelers the brigade encounters and barking orders. A Hellknight brigade is typically based at a keep or other fortification controlling an area measured by a day's ride in every direction—about 25 miles. Farther-ranging missions are possible but require substantial logistical support.

A military serves to defend and fight on behalf of nations and can be trained and deployed in various ways.

action->Troop Movement.name: Troop Movement
action->Troop Movement.description: Whenever a troop moves, you move one of its segments and the other segments follow behind it. At the end of the movement, you can group the other segments adjacent to the one you moved as you see fit, provided none of them moves farther than the moving segment. If you choose not to move the troop any distance, you can instead reshape the position of all the segments as long as one stays in place.

action->Arrow Volley.name: Arrow Volley
action->Arrow Volley.description: The Hellknights draw or reload their longbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 100 feet that deals @Damage[(3d8+2)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic|options:area-effect] save). When the troop is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Boarding Grapple.name: Boarding Grapple
action->Boarding Grapple.description:
Requirements The Hellknights are on a ship

Effect The Hellknights throw a volley of grappling hooks at a 10-foot-radius burst within 30 feet. Five grappling hooks landing at random squares in the burst take hold, and the Hellknights heave, forcibly moving both ships up to 15 feet toward each other until they're adjacent to each other. The ships remain adjacent to each other until all five of the grappling hooks are dislodged (each as an Interact action) or their respective chains are broken (Hardness 8, HP 20, BT 10).

action->Sea Legs.name: Sea Legs
action->Sea Legs.description: The ship crew ignores the penalty for attacking while on a vehicle that has moved in the last round and takes only a –2 penalty if the vehicle is uncontrolled or used an action with the reckless trait.

action->Stab from the Saddle.name: Stab from the Saddle
action->Stab from the Saddle.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The brigade engages in a coordinated lance attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] with a @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d6+7)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d6+14)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(3d6+18)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Give No Ground.name: Give No Ground
action->Give No Ground.description: These soldiers have had it beaten into them that they shall hold their ground no matter what. If any effect would force the House Thrune elite infantry to move, reduce that distance by 10 feet. Any time it would be affected by the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition, the elite infantry is instead @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for the same duration.

action->Hell's Will.name: Hell's Will
action->Hell's Will.description: As long as it has 4 segments, the House Thrune elite infantry troop improves its degree of success on rout checks by one step, even when without a commander. When it has three segments, it instead gains a +2 status bonus to rout checks.

action->Cloven Charge.name: Cloven Charge
action->Cloven Charge.description: The House Thrune elite infantry charges forward with an infernal yell on cloven hooves. The troop Strides up to twice its Speed. At the end of the troop's movement, each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] takes @Damage[(2d6+7)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:34|basic] save.

action->Crossbow Array.name: Crossbow Array
action->Crossbow Array.description: The House Thrune elite infantry unit draws, loads, and fires a barrage from its crossbows. The salvo is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 100 feet that deals @Damage[5d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:34|basic] save). When the elite infantry is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Hellish Spears.name: Hellish Spears
action->Hellish Spears.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The House Thrune elite infantry attacks each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] with its flaming longspears, with a @Check[reflex|dc:34|basic] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(2d6+2)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus @Damage[1d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d6+7)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus @Damage[1d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(7d6+10)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus @Damage[1d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage

weapon->Staff of Control.name: Staff of Control
weapon->Staff of Control.description:
An array of dazzling gemstones lines the twisting head of the golden staff. While wielding the staff, you gain a +1 status bonus to Diplomacy checks to make a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.DCb62iCBrJXy0Ik6]{Request}.

Activate Cast a Spell

Effect You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.

  • Cantrip @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4gBIw4IDrSfFHik4]{Daze}
  • 1st @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.vLA0q0WOK2YPuJs6]{Charm}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aIHY2DArKFweIrpf]{Command}
  • 2nd @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.tlSE7Ly8vi1Dgddv]{Laughing Fit}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.CQb8HtQ1BPeZmu9h]{Stupefy}

Craft Requirements Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells.

armor->+1 Dottari Robes.name: +1 Dottari Robes
armor->+1 Dottari Robes.description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex Cap and can grant an item bonus to AC if etched with potency runes.

equipment->Manacles (Average).name: Manacles (Average)
equipment->Manacles (Average).description: You can manacle someone who is willing or otherwise at your mercy as an exploration activity taking 10-30 seconds depending on the creature's size and how many manacles you apply. A two-legged creature with its legs bound takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds, and a two-handed creature with its wrists bound has to succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] any time it uses a manipulate action or else that action fails. This DC may be higher depending on how tightly the manacles constrain the hands. A creature bound to a stationary object is immobilized. For creatures with more or fewer limbs, the GM determines what effect manacles have, if any. Freeing a creature from average manacles requires four successful @Check[thievery|dc:27|traits:action:pick-a-lock] checks.

action->Hell's Arrest.name: Hell's Arrest
action->Hell's Arrest.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect Ignaro invokes Hell to wrap in chains a creature within 30 feet of him. He attempts to [[/act grapple skill=arcana]] the target using his Arcana modifier in place of Athletics for the check. If successful, the chains keep the target grappled or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} for 1 minute, until the target [[/act escape dc=33]]{Escapes}, or until the chains are destroyed (Hardness 10, 30 HP), whichever comes first.

action->Hell's Own Servitor.name: Hell's Own Servitor
action->Hell's Own Servitor.description: Ignaro is bound to a phistophilus in Erebus. When Ignaro dies, his body disintegrates and his soul is sent to Hell. All weapons that he wields deal an additional @Damage[1d6[spirit]] damage with the unholy trait.

action->Ignite Heresy.name: Ignite Heresy
action->Ignite Heresy.description: Ignaro declares an object he can see within 30 feet to be heretical. After smoldering for 1 round, it's engulfed in flames, taking @Damage[3d6[persistent,fire]] damage that ignores half of the object's Hardness. Immersion in water for 1 round ends the damage, as does the object being splashed with a flask of holy water. Any other water effect that isn't just mundane water used against the object can attempt to counteract the effect (DC 30). If this ability destroys an object, Ignaro gains temporary Hit Points equal to twice the object's level (minimum 2) that last for 1 minute.

Infernal Bow Shocks.name: Infernal Bow Shocks
Infernal Bow Shocks.descriptionHazard: Wave after wave of Hellish power ripples out from the pentagram surrounding the Inferno Gate, bathing all of the battling armies around it in its dark energies. Some are slain on the spot, while others fall into cracks that open beneath them in the earth, and still others are consumed by ignited gasses.
Infernal Bow Shocks.disable:
@Check[Devil Lore|dc:34] or @Check[HellLore|dc:34] to give the proper incantations and invoke the name of Visperthul to ward off the magical defenses from the Inferno Gate; @Check[arcana|dc:38] (expert), @Check[nature|dc:38] (expert), @Check[occultism|dc:38] (expert), or @Check[religion|dc:38] (expert) to erect a magical barrier to shield against the power emanating from the gate; @Check[survival|dc:43] to use the natural terrain as a shield to survive the onslaught of magical energies; @Check[will|dc:48] save to simply push back against the Hellish storm surge and refute them; or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (9th rank; counteract DC 34) to blast the bow shock with magic to stem its flow; three successes are required to dissipate the hazard's energies.

The first time a PC successfully uses Devil Lore or Hell Lore against this hazard, all PCs gain a +2 status bonus to their saving throws against the hazard's effects.
Infernal Bow Shocks.routine: (2 actions) The infernal bow shocks release a different effect each round for 3 rounds, centered on a randomly-chosen PC each time and never the same PC twice in a row (though creatures standing near each other might just be unlucky). In the first round, it unleashes Flaming Fumes. In the second round, it uses an 8th-rank whispers of the void spell (DC 38), and in the third round, it emulates the effect of an earthquake spell (DC 38), with each of the three piles of tower rubble collapsing as though they were shoddy structures. Notably, no fissures extend into the former inner bailey of Fort Arego (area E4, as outlined by the remaining rectangular battlements). The hazard concludes for the PCs when it's disabled, or when the third round ends.

action->Shock Wave.name: Shock Wave
action->Shock Wave.description:
Trigger Encounter E3 begins

Effect A randomly chosen PC is targeted at the center of a @Template[type:burst|distance:20] of acrid-smelling (but invisible) fumes. The hazard unleashes Flaming Fumes on the same target as Shock Wave. A creature must succeed at a Perception check against the hazard's Stealth DC to visually detect the fumes. The infernal bow shocks then roll initiative.

action->Flaming Fumes.name: Flaming Fumes
action->Flaming Fumes.description: A @Template[type:burst|distance:20] of acridsmelling fumes ignites in gouts of hellfire. All creatures in the area take 7d6 fire and @Damage[7d6[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:38|basic] save.

Infernal Tide.name: Infernal Tide
Infernal Tide.publicNotes: Sarglagons can gather to form an infernal tide, a troop that displays unnerving fluidity in the way its devils seem to flow around each other.
Infernal Tide.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Heavy Aura.name: Heavy Aura
action->Heavy Aura.description:
10 feet.

A creature that enters the aura becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.D5mg6Tc7Jzrj6ro7]{Encumbered} while it remains in the aura unless it succeeds at a @Check[will|dc:30] save, after which it's immune for 10 minutes.

action->Stygian Guardian.name: Stygian Guardian
action->Stygian Guardian.description:
Trigger A creature or object within 5 feet of the troop is targeted by an attack or offensive troop ability

Effect Some of the infernal tide interpose themselves, giving the target standard cover against the attack, or greater cover if the troop was already granting it lesser cover.

action->Drown.name: Drown
action->Drown.description:
The infernal tide conjures murky water to fill the lungs of all creatures that can't breathe water within a @Template[type:cone|distance:30]. Each target in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target coughs up water and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The target must hold its breath. The only action it can take is to attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save to expel the water, which is a single action.

Critical Failure The target falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} and begins suffocating. If the target succeeds at its Fortitude save while suffocating, it coughs up the water and can breathe again.

action->Sarglagon Venom.name: Sarglagon Venom
action->Sarglagon Venom.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:33]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} (1 round)

action->Tentacle Onslaught.name: Tentacle Onslaught
action->Tentacle Onslaught.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The troop lashes out their tentacles in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save). The damage depends on the number of actions. A creature that fails its save is also exposed to sarglagon venom.

1 @Damage[(1d8+3)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d8+6)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(5d8+10)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

armor->Explorer's Clothing.name: Explorer's Clothing
armor->Explorer's Clothing.description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex Cap and can grant an item bonus to AC if etched with potency runes.

equipment->Tailoring Toolkit (Sterling).name: Tailoring Toolkit (Sterling)
equipment->Tailoring Toolkit (Sterling).description:
You need these tools to create items from raw materials with the Craft skill. Sterling artisan's tools give you a +1 item bonus to the check.

Different sets are needed for different work, as determined by the GM; for example, a blacksmith's toolkit differs from a woodworker's toolkit. If you wear your artisan's toolkit, you can draw and replace it as part of the action that uses it.

equipment->Crown of Intellect.name: Crown of Intellect
equipment->Crown of Intellect.description:
A trio of brilliant gems have been set into this elegant golden crown. You gain a +3 item bonus to checks to Recall Knowledge, regardless of the skill. When you invest the cloak, you either increase your Intelligence modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. This gives you additional trained skills and languages, as normal for increasing your Intelligence modifier. You must select skills and languages the first time you invest the crown, and whenever you invest the same crown of intellect, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

Activate—Search Your Mind 1 (concentrate)

Frequency once per hour

Effect You gain the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.ZYoC630tNGutgbE0]{Hypercognition}.

action->Search Your Mind.name: Search Your Mind
action->Search Your Mind.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect You gain the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.ZYoC630tNGutgbE0]{Hypercognition}.

action->Sartorial Snag.name: Sartorial Snag
action->Sartorial Snag.description:
Trigger An adjacent creature uses a manipulate action or a move action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using

Effect The disciple drives several threaded needles through the triggering creature's clothing and pulls, momentarily binding its outfit and restricting movement. The disciple attempts a @Check[Tailoring Lore] check against the triggering creature's AC. If the triggering creature isn't wearing at least a cloak, sash, tabard, or similar amount of visible cloth, apply a –2 circumstance penalty to this check.

Critical Success As success, plus the disciple disrupts the action.

Success Threads partly entangle the triggering creature, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to it Speeds until the beginning of its next turn. Any creature within reach of the targeted creature can end these effects with an Interact action.

Failure The triggering creature is unaffected.

Critical Failure The disciple becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the beginning of their next turn.

action->Sequester.name: Sequester
action->Sequester.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect Kaisentia instantly transports herself and any items she's carrying to area B9, as if casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.VlNcjmYyu95vOUe8]{Translocate} as a 9th-rank spell with a maximum range of 5 miles.

action->Pins and Needles.name: Pins and Needles
action->Pins and Needles.description: The disciple gains access to the critical specialization effects of their sleeve and needle Strikes, with a DC of 34.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The disciple's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Tailor-Made Armaments.name: Tailor-Made Armaments
action->Tailor-Made Armaments.description: While wearing clothing worth at least 10 gp, a disciple of thread can cause their sleeves or other parts of their outfit to extend and animate as unarmed Strikes in the flail weapon group. While wielding these sleeves, the cultist can use their Tailoring Lore modifier in place of Athletics to [[/act grapple skill=tailoring-lore]], [[/act reposition skill=tailoring-lore]], and [[/act trip skill=tailoring-lore]]. As a free action while carrying a tailoring toolkit, the disciple can Draw mundane sewing needles to use as lethal throwing weapons in the bow weapon group.

weapon->Warhammer (Shattered).name: Warhammer (Shattered)
weapon->Warhammer (Shattered).description: This weapon has a wooden shaft ending in a large, heavy metal head. The head of the hammer might be single-sided or double-sided, but it's always capable of delivering powerful bludgeoning blows.

weapon->Mace.name: Mace
weapon->Mace.description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and allows its wielder to deliver powerful blows and dent armor.

armor->Hellknight Breastplate.name: Hellknight Breastplate
armor->Hellknight Breastplate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight breastplate serves those in the order who lack the training to wear heavy armor.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

equipment->Religious Symbol of Asmodeus.name: Religious Symbol of Asmodeus
equipment->Religious Symbol of Asmodeus.description: This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol can be worn on the body on a chain or pin, or can be held.

action->Torag's Apostate.name: Torag's Apostate
action->Torag's Apostate.description: Liabriz only recently forswore Torag. While he carries his shattered warhammer, he gains a +1 status bonus on saves versus Torag and those that gain divine power from him. If Liabriz fails a save against Torag or one of his servitors, Liabriz critically fails instead.

action->Channeling Block.name: Channeling Block
action->Channeling Block.description: When Liabriz Shield Blocks, he can expend a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz]{Harm} spell. He rolls 6d8 and increases his shield's Hardness by that total for the block.

Majordomo Cizoboseth.name: Majordomo Cizoboseth
Majordomo Cizoboseth.publicNotes:
Where a bright mind seeking knowledge becomes corrupted by eloquent lies and twisted truths, an ayngavhaul is sure to lurk. Often donned in the holy vestments of other faiths in a twisted mockery of their principles, these wily, well-read devils delight in using their knowledge to twist religious texts and teachings into the dangerous principles those same texts warn against. While many ayngavhauls develop advanced knowledge of unique specializations, there are many whose depth of knowledge of a single religion rivals that of even the most wizened priests.

Any information an ayngavhaul has read or learned is added to a massive living tome unique to the ayngavhaul. The information contained sorts itself based on the topic and point its owner is making.

While these devils spend most of their time in the libraries of Hell, they are most often summoned in desperate bids by students seeking lost or forbidden knowledge, and such knowledge comes with a cost. These devils use truths and loose interpretations of texts to lend credence to their heretical viewpoints. When these once-bright intellectuals are corrupted into blasphemous priests, tyrants, and despots spreading the twisted words of an ayngavhaul, that devil gains influence and renown within the scholarly circles of Hell.

action->Personal Library.name: Personal Library
action->Personal Library.description: Cizoboseth keeps records of palace events and accounts in a personal archive, a floating collection of ledgers constantly revolving around him. Retrieving or returning a ledger requires an Interact action.

action->Spellblock Ledger.name: Spellblock Ledger
action->Spellblock Ledger.description:
Trigger Cizoboseth is targeted by a spell

Effect Cizoboseth flings a ledger from his personal archive at the spell. He attempts a @Check[flat|dc:5]. On a success, the ledger fully absorbs the effects of the spell and burns up into a harmless pile of ash. Regardless of the result, Cizoboseth can't use this ability again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge this ability]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Herald Heresy.name: Herald Heresy
action->Herald Heresy.description:
Cizoboseth imparts blasphemous thoughts into the minds of all non-devil creatures within a @Template[type:burst|distance:20] up to 60 feet away. An affected creature takes @Damage[3d10[mental]|options:area-damage] damage plus @Damage[3d10[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:37] save. Affected creatures gain a cumulative +1 circumstance bonus (up to a total of +4) to saves against all future attempts to Herald Heresy for 1 minute, as they become inured to the blasphemies.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 hour.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2}.

action->Poison Minds.name: Poison Minds
action->Poison Minds.description: Creatures hit by Cizoboseth's searing words must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:37] saving throw or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure). If the target is trained in Religion, it takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its save.

weapon->+2 Greater Striking Corundum (Hi.name: +2 Greater Striking Corundum (High-Grade ) Flail
weapon->+2 Greater Striking Corundum (Hi.description: This weapon consists of a wooden handle attached to a spiked ball or cylinder by a chain, rope, or strap of leather.

shield->Corundum (High-Grade) Shield.name: Corundum (High-Grade) Shield
shield->Corundum (High-Grade) Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

Hardness

HP

BT

8

32

16

armor->Explorer's Clothing.name: Explorer's Clothing
armor->Explorer's Clothing.description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex Cap and can grant an item bonus to AC if etched with potency runes.

action->Extol.name: Extol
action->Extol.description: Marala hefts her shield and orders a strix ally to protect themselves as well. Marala Raises a Shield, then causes one other strix ally within 30 feet to also Raise a Shield as a free action.

action->Glorify.name: Glorify
action->Glorify.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect Marala invokes the valor of her strix allies. Up to four strix allies within 30 feet can Step, Stride, or Fly up to their Speed toward an enemy as a free action. If she used 2 actions to activate this ability, up to two of the affected strix of Marala's choice can then Strike an adjacent enemy as a reaction.

action->Ward Contract.name: Ward Contract
action->Ward Contract.description: A signed contract carried by Mareshiah (including draped over her horns) is immune to damage from all creatures other than her. Mareshiah is immune to mental effects that would make them destroy, nullify, or alter a contract.

action->Draft Contract.name: Draft Contract
action->Draft Contract.description:
Mareshiah produces an infernal contract for a single living mortal. This contract can grant a wide range of abilities and effects, akin to the power of a wish ritual but fulfilled to the letter by Mareshiah. To receive any of those benefits, the mortal must willingly sign their true name to the contract. At that point, the mortal's soul is bound to Mareshiah and Hell.

While the contract is in effect, the victim can't be restored to life except by a wish ritual or similar magic. If the mortal is restored to life by those means, Mareshiah knows which mortal came to life and can locate the creature or creatures who restored the mortal to life for 1 year, gaining the effects of a pinpoint spell. Avoiding the terms of an infernal contract is difficult and often dangerous.

action->Infernal Investment.name: Infernal Investment
action->Infernal Investment.description: Mareshiah can cast a 10th-rank innate @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.r784cIz17eWujtQj]{Scrying} spell at will, but only to target a creature with which they have a contract. The target automatically critically fails its save.

action->Infernal Wound.name: Infernal Wound
action->Infernal Wound.description:
The wounds from Mareshiah's Strikes resist healing.

A spellcaster or item attempting to use healing magic on a creature damaged by Mareshiah's Strikes must first attempt to counteract infernal wound (DC 42). If it isn't counteracted, the healing has no effect.

weapon->Mace.name: Mace
weapon->Mace.description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and allows its wielder to deliver powerful blows and dent armor.

armor->Explorer's Clothing.name: Explorer's Clothing
armor->Explorer's Clothing.description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex Cap and can grant an item bonus to AC if etched with potency runes.

action->Contractual Lifespan.name: Contractual Lifespan
action->Contractual Lifespan.description: Meliar Aructus takes @Damage[25[untyped]] damage at the end of his turn if he Cast a Spell, used Borrow Time, or made a Strike that turn. This damage ignores resistance.

action->Infernal Tendrils.name: Infernal Tendrils
action->Infernal Tendrils.description:
30 feet.

When a creature in the aura would be healed by a vitality effect, the healing is reduced by 15, and Meliar Aructus regains @Damage[15[healing]] Hit Points.

action->Mark of the Devil.name: Mark of the Devil
action->Mark of the Devil.description: Whoever kills Meliar must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:35] save or be visibly marked as anathema to Asmodeus. They gain weakness 10 to unholy damage and take a –2 circumstance penalty to Charisma-based skill checks against followers of Asmodeus. The DCs of checks to [[/act gather-information]] about or [[/act track]] the marked creature are reduced by 2. The mark can't be hidden.

action->Shattered Vessel.name: Shattered Vessel
action->Shattered Vessel.description: When Meliar dies, the divine power barely contained within his body explodes outward, dealing @Damage[6d8[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic] save).

action->Borrow Time.name: Borrow Time
action->Borrow Time.description: Meliar chooses two different creatures in his infernal tendrils aura. Each one must be either a devil or himself. One target loses 25 HP, and the other regains that many HP. Unwilling targets can negate the transfer with a successful @Check[fortitude|dc:39] save.

action->Chill of Cocytus.name: Chill of Cocytus
action->Chill of Cocytus.description: Meliar's unarmed attacks and mace gain the +2 greater striking decaying frost runes when used by him, and his Strikes gain the death trait.

Merztorlegon.name: Merztorlegon
Merztorlegon.publicNotes: Merztorlegons are walking siege engines, their bodies bristling with ballistae and siege towers bolted to their shoulders. Their frontal armor leaves their back less defended, forcing Hell's commanders to support these juggernauts with infantry that can shield them from flanking strikes.
Merztorlegon.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Arrow Volley.name: Arrow Volley
action->Arrow Volley.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The vordines fire projectiles, dealing @Damage[7d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage to creatures in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 200 feet (@Check[reflex|dc:38|basic] save).

action->Besieging Fury.name: Besieging Fury
action->Besieging Fury.description: A merztorlegon and their crew can [[/act demoralize]] creatures at a range of 120 feet and don't take a penalty for not sharing a language with the target.

action->Shoulder Ballista.name: Shoulder Ballista
action->Shoulder Ballista.description:
A shoulder ballista is an extension of the merztorlegon's body, functioning as a siege weapon that shares the demon's space, can't be targeted, and ceases to function after the merztorlegon's death. Either the merztorlegon or their siege crew can Aim or Load the ballista, but only the crew can Launch it.

Aim 1 80 feet, minimum distance 30 feet

Load 1 (manipulate) 1 time

Launch 1 (attack, magical, manipulate, range increment 120 feet, unholy) 8d12 piercing, single target, @Check[reflex|dc:41]

action->Siege Crew.name: Siege Crew
action->Siege Crew.description: Several vordines inhabit the siege tower and are an extension of the merztorlegon, use the devil's skill modifiers, and can't be targeted individually. On the siege devil's turn, the crew can take 2 actions that can be used only to launch an Arrow Volley; [[/act demoralize]]; or to Aim, Load, or Launch the devil's shoulder ballista. Once the merztorlegon is reduced to half their maximum Hit Points, the siege crew is injured and takes only 1 action per turn.

action->Sunder Structures.name: Sunder Structures
action->Sunder Structures.description: A merztorlegon's melee Strikes deal an additional @Damage[3d12[untyped]] damage to items and structures.

action->Troop Transport.name: Troop Transport
action->Troop Transport.description: The merztorlegon gathers a willing adjacent troop into their siege tower or shunts a stored troop into an adjacent space. The devil can store only one troop at a time, during which time the troop can't take actions, can't be targeted by effects, can be transported by the devil's translocate spell, and increases the damage dealt by Arrow Volley by 2d8. If the merztorlegon is defeated, any stored troop immediately reappears in or adjacent to the siege devil's space.

action->Dance Partner.name: Dance Partner
action->Dance Partner.description:
Trigger An adjacent creature whom the mannequin dealt damage to with a fist Strike since the beginning of the mannequin's last turn attempts to move away from the mannequin

Effect The mannequin dances after the triggering creature, either Climbing or Striding, following the creature and staying in reach throughout its movement until the creature stops moving or the mannequin has moved up to its full Speed, whichever happens first. The mannequin gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and Reflex saves against reactions triggered by its movement. If it critically succeeds at a Reflex save against a reaction triggered by its movement (or if the attacker critically fails its attack roll or check), the mannequin maneuvers the creature it's following so that the latter is exposed to the reaction; the attacker rerolls the attack roll or check against the followed creature or forces the followed creature to attempt a Reflex save against the reaction instead.

action->Accessorized.name: Accessorized
action->Accessorized.description: The mannequin wears accessories (typically three) that it can Draw as a free action and use as thrown weapons. The type of damage varies by the accessory, such as slashing damage for a wide-brimmed hat, piercing for a stiletto-heeled shoe, or bludgeoning for a handbag.

Nemyra.name: Nemyra
Nemyra.publicNotes:
Also called a hamadryad, a dryad queen rules over an entire forest, or a portion of an incredibly large forest, leading and protecting all dryads within. Dryad queens often have strange relationships with powerful and deadly fey, working together in a dualistic way despite their differences, with the queen representing nature's wonders and the other fey representing nature's wrath.

Nymphs are a family of fey that take the form of beautiful humanoids with elven features and have a deep association with the natural world. The most common of their kind are the dryads, which are spirits that embody great trees, but many other kinds of nymphs exist, including naiads, who watch over bodies of water. All nymphs are guardians of some element of nature, typically a specific tree or pond, or even-in the case of nymph queens-whole forests or massive bodies of water

Nymph Queens

Nymph queens are powerful nymphs who rule over entire regions of untouched wilderness, not just single trees or ponds. Every variety of nymph can have a queen. Naiad queens are among the most prominent, and more often interact with nearby mortals. Thus, some scholars refer to naiad queens as simply "nymphs."

action->Nature Empathy.name: Nature Empathy
action->Nature Empathy.description: The dryad can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with animals and plants.

action->Tied to the Sea.name: Tied to the Sea
action->Tied to the Sea.description: Nemyra is tied to the kelp forest in this room and treats the kelp as a tree for the purposes of her spells and abilities. Creatures that she summons with her @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.jSRAyd57kd4WZ4yE]{Summon Plant or Fungus} spell have the aquatic trait and a swim speed of 30 feet.

action->Nymph's Beauty.name: Nymph's Beauty
action->Nymph's Beauty.description:
30 feet. On a failed save, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} in awe for 1 minute.

Creatures that start their turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:30|options:area-effect,inflicts:immobilized] save or suffer an effect described in the nymph queen's entry.

action->Change Shape.name: Change Shape
action->Change Shape.description:
Nymph queens can transform between their original form, which looks much like a typical nymph of their kind, and any Small or Medium humanoid form, typically choosing a more humanoid-looking version of their natural form.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

action->Focus Beauty.name: Focus Beauty
action->Focus Beauty.description:
On a failed save, if the target was already affected by the dryad queen's beauty, the target can't use hostile actions against the dryad queen for 1 hour.

The nymph queen focuses her beauty upon a target, who must attempt a save against her nymph's beauty aura (@Check[will|dc:30] save). If the creature fails and was already affected by the aura, it takes a greater effect described in the nymph queen's entry.

A nymph queen can Focus Beauty on a given creature only once per turn.

action->Inspiration.name: Inspiration
action->Inspiration.description:
The nymph queen inspires a single intelligent creature by giving that creature a token of her favor, typically a lock of her hair, though it can be some other significant object as well. As long as the creature carries her token and remains in good standing with her, the creature gains a +1 status bonus to all Crafting checks, Performance checks, and Will saves.

If the nymph grants her token to a bard, and she's the bard's muse, the queen chooses one additional benefit granted by her token: a +1 status bonus to all Lore checks, a +2 status bonus to Performance checks when determining the effects of compositions, a +4 status bonus to untrained skill checks, or a +2 status bonus to Will saves against fey.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.iSyyR4QVOaY6mxtg]{Effect: Nymph Queen's Inspiration}

action->Tree Meld.name: Tree Meld
action->Tree Meld.description:
A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.dileJ0Yxqg76LMvu]{One with Plants} spell cast by a dryad has an unlimited duration.

In addition, if the dryad merges with any tree in her domain, she can choose to instead enter an extradimensional living space within the tree, and can bring up to eight adjacent, willing creatures with her; the spell gains the extradimensional trait. The dryad can still be expelled from this space.

Nyvorlas Yandor.name: Nyvorlas Yandor
Nyvorlas Yandor.publicNotes:
Whenever high command needs an enemy spellcaster taken off the board in the midst of battle, they send in a mage killer.

Whether they're hired to wage war, protect a caravan, or infiltrate an impenetrable fortress, there's ample work for mercenaries all over Golarion.

weapon->Dagger.name: Dagger
weapon->Dagger.description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

weapon->Rapier.name: Rapier
weapon->Rapier.description: The rapier is a long and thin piercing blade with a basket hilt. It is prized among many as a dueling weapon.

armor->Studded Leather Armor.name: Studded Leather Armor
armor->Studded Leather Armor.description: This leather armor is reinforced with metal studs and sometimes small metal plates, providing most of the flexibility of leather armor with more robust protection.

action->Spell Dodge.name: Spell Dodge
action->Spell Dodge.description:
Trigger The mage killer is targeted by a spell

Effect The mage killer gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against the triggering spell.

action->Spell Interception.name: Spell Interception
action->Spell Interception.description:
Trigger A creature within 10 feet of the mage killer Casts a Spell

Effect The mage killer makes a melee Strike or thrown dagger Strike against the triggering creature. If it hits, the spell is disrupted.

action->Magical Static.name: Magical Static
action->Magical Static.description: The mage killer's Strikes deal an additional 1d8+2 mental damage to a creature that has Cast (or attempted to Cast) a Spell within the last round, and on a critical hit, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute.

action->Shift Energy Runes.name: Shift Energy Runes
action->Shift Energy Runes.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect The mage killer alters the magical countermeasures in the runes on their armor. They change their resistance to the energy type of their choice (acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, sonic, vitality, or void).

action->Squad's Spell Dodge.name: Squad's Spell Dodge
action->Squad's Spell Dodge.description: When Nyvorlas uses her Spell Dodge reaction, any troop she commands also gains the saving throw bonus against the triggering spell.

action->Squad's Static.name: Squad's Static
action->Squad's Static.description: Any troop Nyvorlas commands also gains her magical static ability, applying its effects to her troop's offensive abilities.

action->Sudden Charge.name: Sudden Charge
action->Sudden Charge.description: The mage killer Strides twice. If they end their movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, they can make a melee Strike against it.

Oath Howler.name: Oath Howler
Oath Howler.publicNotes:
Sometimes the wind catches a profane oath as it's uttered, carrying it far away before the blasphemy can form a tortured eddy that repeats the curse again and again. Such winds eventually tie themselves in a knot and take on a sinister intelligence.

The unholy negotiations Abrogail I held with Hell deep in the Whisperwood spun off an untold number of oath howlers, most of whom haunt that forest and its skies to this day. Oath howlers delight in other creatures' profanities. A potential victim can distract the elementals indefinitely so long as the speaker varies their languages, insults, and intonation. If done artfully, a creature might sandwich a request between pejoratives and so earn an oath howler's morbid cooperation—at least so long as they continue encouraging the elemental with fresh profane oaths.
Oath Howler.allSavesBonus: +2 status to all saves vs. auditory and sonic

action->Oathsense.name: Oathsense
action->Oathsense.description: The oath howler can hear profanities—anything from foul language to someone casting an unholy spell that lacks the subtle trait—uttered within 1 mile, after which they can detect the speaker as a vague sense for 1 minute (or as an imprecise sense within 100 feet). The oath howler can understand profanities in any language, including several words that precede and follow the profanity for context, and thereafter can mimic and repeat those profanities.

action->+2 Status to All Saves vs. Audit.name: +2 Status to All Saves vs. Auditory and Sonic

action->Rebuking Smoke.name: Rebuking Smoke
action->Rebuking Smoke.description:
Trigger A creature within 30 feet damages the oath howler

Effect The oath howler's wound belches foul smoke onto the triggering creature, who must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save. The creature is then immune to that oath howler's Rebuking Smoke for 1 minute.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is dazzled for 1 round and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature is sickened 1 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} until the sickened condition ends.

action->Storm of Blasphemies.name: Storm of Blasphemies
action->Storm of Blasphemies.description:
The oath howler roars in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30], shouting dozens of pejoratives in as many languages. Creatures in the area take @Damage[10d8[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:33|basic] save). Creatures that critically fail also take @Damage[2d8[persistent,sonic]|options:area-damage] damage and are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} until the persistent damage ends.

The oath howler can't use this ability again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge this ability]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Swiftness.name: Swiftness
action->Swiftness.description: The oath howler's movement doesn't trigger reactions.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

weapon->Mace.name: Mace
weapon->Mace.description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and allows its wielder to deliver powerful blows and dent armor.

shield->Steel Shield.name: Steel Shield
shield->Steel Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

HardnessHPBT
52010

armor->Full Plate.name: Full Plate
armor->Full Plate.description: Plate mail consists of interlocking plates that encase nearly the entire body in a carapace of steel. It is costly and heavy, and the wearer often requires help to don it correctly, but it provides some of the best defense armor can supply. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.zBYEU9E7034ENCmh]{Padded Armor} and a pair of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Ix2vicchE79d6Cl3]{gauntlets}.

equipment->Religious Symbol (Silver).name: Religious Symbol (Silver)
equipment->Religious Symbol (Silver).description: This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol can be worn on the body on a chain or pin, or can be held.

action->Unholy Shield.name: Unholy Shield
action->Unholy Shield.description: In Obrimar's hands, his shield gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.bvROhtzAez0KP7Vt]{moderate reinforcing} rune, giving it Hardness 8, 84 HP, and BT 42.

action->Champion's Aura.name: Champion's Aura
action->Champion's Aura.description:
15 feet.

Any follower of Asmodeus in the aura knows Obrimar is a champion of Asmodeus. At the end of Obrimar's turn, each ally in the aura reduces their frightened value by 1. The aura can be suppressed or resumed with a single action, which has the concentrate trait, and ends if Obrimar falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}.

action->Exalted Iron Command.name: Exalted Iron Command
action->Exalted Iron Command.description:
Trigger An enemy in Obrimar's champion's aura damages him

Effect The creature that damaged Obrimar can choose to drop @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} as a free action. If it refuses, it takes @Damage[4d6[mental]] damage and 4 persistent spirit damage. All other enemies within Obrimar's champion's aura must also drop prone as a free action or else take @Damage[4[mental]] damage if they refuse.

action->Infernal Courage.name: Infernal Courage
action->Infernal Courage.description: When Obrimar becomes frightened, he reduces the condition value by 1 (to a minimum of 0).

action->Infernal Reactions.name: Infernal Reactions
action->Infernal Reactions.description: At the start of each of his turns, Obrimar gains an additional reaction that he can only use to make a Reactive Strike or Shield Block.

action->Never Fall.name: Never Fall
action->Never Fall.description:
Trigger Obrimar's Hit Points are reduced to 0 for the first time that day

Effect Obrimar refuses to give up his soul to Asmodeus just yet. He remains standing with @Damage[25[healing]] Hit Points.

action->Never Falter.name: Never Falter
action->Never Falter.description: Obrimar declares his devotion to Asmodeus and the cause of obedience. He Strides, then makes a melee Strike. If the Strike hits an enemy, all allies within his champion's aura gain a +2 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws against fear until the start of Obrimar's next turn.

Ochiastis.name: Ochiastis
Ochiastis.publicNotes: While Hell prefers to subjugate mortals by force, cow them with majesty, or bind them through contracts, even the most principled archdevil sometimes needs an assassin. In their natural form, an ochiastis resembles a nephilim with a narrow mane of venomous quills running from the top of their head to the small of their back. This fiend is a capable shapeshifter, yet their quills remain no matter the shape they take—a drawback an ochiastis tries to keep hidden by wearing a cloak or other bulky clothing. When not on missions, an ochiastis often lurks in urban areas, familiarizing themself with local behaviors and shortcuts.
Ochiastis.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Assassin's Pursuit.name: Assassin's Pursuit
action->Assassin's Pursuit.description:
Trigger The ochiastis's hunted prey is within their reach and attempts to move away from them

Effect The ochiastis Climbs or Strides up to their Speed, following their hunted prey and keeping it in reach throughout its movement until it stops moving or the ochiastis has moved their full Speed.

action->Faceless Escape.name: Faceless Escape
action->Faceless Escape.description:
Trigger The ochiastis takes damage

Requirements The ochiastis is in or adjacent to a crowd of at least 10 people

Effect The ochiastis attempts to [[/act hide]] by blending into the crowd, even if the devil lacks cover or concealment. The ochiastis can then [[/act sneak]]. Each time the ochiastis becomes hidden from a creature in this way, that creature gains a cumulative +2 circumstance bonus to its Perception DC for 1 hour against the ochiastis's Stealth checks when using Faceless Escape.

action->Change Shape.name: Change Shape
action->Change Shape.description:
The ochiastis can take the appearance of any Small or Medium humanoid creature. This doesn't change the ochiastis's Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their claw Strike but might change the damage type this Strike deals (typically to bludgeoning). They lose their quill Strike. Creatures that [[/act grapple]] the ochiastis or otherwise touch their back gain a +4 circumstance bonus to see through their disguise.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

action->Hunt Prey.name: Hunt Prey
action->Hunt Prey.description:
You designate a single creature as your prey and focus your attacks against that creature. You must be able to see or hear the prey, or you must be tracking the prey during exploration.

You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks when you @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} your prey and a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks when you @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.EA5vuSgJfiHH7plD]{Track} your prey. You also ignore the penalty for making ranged attacks within your second range increment against the prey you're hunting.

You can have only one creature designated as your prey at a time. If you use Hunt Prey against a creature when you already have a creature designated, the prior creature loses the designation and the new prey gains the designation. Your designation lasts until your next daily preparations.

action->Ochiastis Venom.name: Ochiastis Venom
action->Ochiastis Venom.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:36]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} (1 round)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} and off-guard (1 round)

Stage 3 enfeebled 2, off-guard, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (1 round)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} (1 round)

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The creature's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

weapon->Greataxe.name: Greataxe
weapon->Greataxe.description: This large battle axe is too heavy to wield with only one hand. Many greataxes incorporate two blades, and they are often "bearded," having a hook at the bottom to increase the strength of their chopping power.

armor->Hide Armor.name: Hide Armor
armor->Hide Armor.description: A mix of furs, sturdy hide, and sometimes molded boiled leather, this armor provides protection due to its layers of leather, though its bulkiness slows the wearer down and decreases mobility.

equipment->Eternal Eruption of Droskar's.name: Eternal Eruption of Droskar's Crag
equipment->Eternal Eruption of Droskar's.description:
Resembling frozen lava, an eternal eruption is made with the same type of time magic, but is built to loop through time, reforming itself after it's used. Determining the difference between the two requires a close examination to see a faint, repeating pattern of red runes.

Activate—Lava Bomb 2 (concentrate, manipulate)

Effect You fling the eternal eruption, with the effect of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.YS7LJSvooG7mfwH1]{Frozen Lava of Droskar's Crag}. After [[/br 2d4 #hours]]{2d4 hours}, the eternal eruption reforms itself in a container on your person, typically the one you most recently stored it in.

Eternal Eruption of Droskar's Crag deals @Damage[13d6[fire]|options:area-damage] on a @Check[reflex|dc:32|basic|options:area-effect].

action->Doorway Traveler.name: Doorway Traveler
action->Doorway Traveler.description: Oourax has journeyed through 13 red-tinged doorways in Narona and drawn power from them, granting her 130 temporary Hit Points for 1 week. For each Narona doorway that the PCs have sealed, reduce this amount by 10.

action->Flash of Insight.name: Flash of Insight
action->Flash of Insight.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger Oourax is about to roll a d20

Effect Oourax peers into an occluded spectrum of possible futures. She gets a success (but not a critical success) on the roll instead of rolling.

action->Hellfire Magic.name: Hellfire Magic
action->Hellfire Magic.description: Oourax can shape her spells into hellfire. Whenever she casts chain lightning, fireball, ignition, or wall of fire, instead of its normal damage, she can have the spell deal half its damage as fire damage and half as spirit damage.

action->Lava Bomb.name: Lava Bomb
action->Lava Bomb.description:
