You fling the eternal eruption, with the effect of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.YS7LJSvooG7mfwH1]{Frozen Lava of Droskar's Crag}. After [[/br 2d4 #hours]]{2d4 hours}, the eternal eruption reforms itself in a container on your person, typically the one you most recently stored it in.

Eternal Eruption of Droskar's Crag deals @Damage[13d6[fire]|options:area-damage] on a @Check[reflex|dc:32|basic|options:area-effect].

Page and Chapter.name: Page and Chapter
Page and Chapter.allSavesBonus: +2 status to all saves vs. linguistic effects

action->Mage Bond.name: Mage Bond
action->Mage Bond.description: After performing a 1-hour ritual, a scalescribe can bond with one willing creature capable of Casting Spells. The scalescribe can Aid their bonded caster in Crafting a scroll, and they can make their Transcribed spell available to the caster so long as the spell is of the caster's tradition. A bonded prepared caster can prepare the spell. A bonded spontaneous caster can add the spell to their spell repertoire during their daily preparations, and it remains as long as the Transcribed scroll exists.

action->+2 Status to All Saves vs. Lingu.name: +2 Status to All Saves vs. Linguistic Effects

action->Transcribe.name: Transcribe
action->Transcribe.description:
Trigger Another creature targets the scalescribe with a spell requiring verbal components

Effect The scalescribe attempts to copy the incantation onto a scroll by attempting a counteract check (counteract rank 8, counteract modifier [[/r 1d20+28 #Counteract]]{+28}) with the following effects instead of the normal effects of counteracting. The spell still affects the scalescribe, regardless of the results of this check. The scalescribe can Cast a Spell from any scroll they Transcribe as if it were on their spell list. Transcribing a new scroll renders any previously Transcribed scroll inert.

Critical Success If the triggering spell's rank was no more than 2 higher than the scalescribe's counteract rank, the scalescribe captures a copy of the spell in a scroll that appears in their hand. This scroll can be used normally but fades after 24 hours if not used before then.

Success As critical success, but only if the spell's rank is no more than 1 higher than the scalescribe's counteract rank.

Failure As critical success, but only if the spell's rank is lower than the scalescribe's counteract rank.

Critical Failure The scalescribe fails to copy the spell.

action->Inkstain.name: Inkstain
action->Inkstain.description: When the scalescribe deals damage to a creature with their fountain pen, the creature takes @Damage[4d4[persistent,poison]] damage. When a creature taking this damage attempts to take an action with the concentrate trait, it must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or the action is disrupted.

Pain Coral.name: Pain Coral
Pain Coral.disable: @Check[nature|dc:33] or @Check[occultism|dc:37] or @Check[thievery|dc:37] to pinpoint and disable key synaptic nerves throughout the coral, @Check[religion|dc:37] to quell the coral's tortured memories; each success reduces the coral's number of actions per turn by 1, and the hazard is disabled when after four successes.
Pain Coral.reset: The coral diffuses and resets after 1 minute.
Pain Coral.routine: (4 actions) The coral casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.R8bqnYiThB6MYTxD]{Phantom Pain} as a 6th-rank spell on a target (DC 33), targeting creatures that aren't already taking persistent mental damage.

action->Surge of Pain.name: Surge of Pain
action->Surge of Pain.description:
Trigger A creature takes damage within area D4

Effect The brain coral casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.yHujiDQPdtXW797e]{Spirit Link} on Xendra. The hazard rolls initiative.

action->Share the Sadism.name: Share the Sadism
action->Share the Sadism.description:
Trigger A creature within 30 feet of the coral takes persistent mental damage

Effect The coral grants an equal number of temporary Hit Points to a different creature within 30 feet.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

weapon->Mace.name: Mace
weapon->Mace.description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and allows its wielder to deliver powerful blows and dent armor.

shield->Sturdy Shield (Greater).name: Sturdy Shield (Greater)
shield->Sturdy Shield (Greater).description:
With a superior design and excellent craftsmanship, this steel shield has higher Hardness than its non-magical counterparts, making it harder to break and destroy.

The shield has Hardness 15, HP 120, and BT 60.

armor->Breastplate.name: Breastplate
armor->Breastplate.description: Though referred to as a breastplate, this type of armor consists of several pieces of plate or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.pRoikbRo5HFW6YUB]{Half Plate} armor that protect the torso, chest, neck, and sometimes the hips and lower legs. It strategically grants some of the protection of plate while allowing greater flexibility and speed.

action->Hellish Determination.name: Hellish Determination
action->Hellish Determination.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The palace guard's training allows them to shrug off debilitating effects. The palace guard chooses a nonpermanent condition affecting them. That condition ends. This doesn't heal any Hit Points already lost from the condition, and it can't remove conditions occurring automatically due to the palace guard's situation or positioning (such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} or flanked).

action->Vicious Swing.name: Vicious Swing
action->Vicious Swing.description: The palace guard makes a melee Strike. This counts as two attacks for the palace guard's multiple attack penalty. If this Strike hits, they deal two extra dice of weapon damage.

Pure Legion Squad.name: Pure Legion Squad
Pure Legion Squad.publicNotes: Soldiers who participate in multiple campaigns of war gain the experience necessary to become their military's finest fighting forces. First-class infantry troops are deployed on battlefields where winning the day is of the utmost importance.

action->Cloak of Purity.name: Cloak of Purity
action->Cloak of Purity.description: The Pure Legion receives a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against spells and effects with the divine, holy, or unholy traits. If commanded by a character who can cast divine spells, the Pure Legion squad takes a –2 circumstance penalty to all rout checks.

action->No Retreat.name: No Retreat
action->No Retreat.description: These soldiers have been extensively trained to hold their ground no matter the situation. If any effect would force the pure legion squad to move, reduce the distance by 10 feet. Any time they would be affected by the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition, the pure legion squad is instead @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for the same duration.

action->Bolt Salvo.name: Bolt Salvo
action->Bolt Salvo.description: The pure legion squad draws, loads, and shoots a salvo from their crossbows. The salvo is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 120 feet that deals @Damage[4d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save). When the pure legion squad is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Clash of Steel.name: Clash of Steel
action->Clash of Steel.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The pure legion squad attacks each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(2d6+1)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d6+10)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(4d6+18)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Drilled in Formations.name: Drilled in Formations
action->Drilled in Formations.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The pure legion squad uses @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.USyDPcSdzcm024zT]{Change Formation}. A pure legion squad unit typically knows the marching column and wedge formations.

action->First-class Charge.name: First-class Charge
action->First-class Charge.description: The pure legion squad rushes forward with a hunger for battle. They Stride up to twice their Speed. At the end of their movement, each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] takes @Damage[(2d6+5)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage, with a @Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save.

Quelaunt.name: Quelaunt
Quelaunt.publicNotes:
This three-armed, three-legged monster has no discernible eyes, nose, ears, or mouth, and no visible means of ingesting food. Its limbs are distributed so evenly across its body that it's all but impossible to tell which way the creature is oriented at any given time. Few who witness a quelaunt linger on its alien looks for long though, as the invasion of their minds becomes a more pressing concern, sowing doubt, sorrow, and rage. This monstrosity not only delights in the negative emotions of its prey, but feeds on them, gaining strength and sustenance from their dismay. For the quelaunt, no act is too terrible or cruel to inflict on its victims, since the more a creature suffers, the more the quelaunt feasts.

Quelaunts are known to associate with other aberrant horrors, including jah-tohls and other creatures associated with the Dominion of the Black. However, the more prevalent theory paints them as more alien—perhaps invasive beings from another dimension of pure thought and feeling—and claims that in their natural state they have no physical bodies at all. Few dare speculate further; the only known autopsy of a quelaunt resulted in the researcher's suicide just days afterward and all the notes were mysteriously destroyed. Whatever secrets there are to be unlocked in the anatomy of these bizarre monsters are apparently important enough to warrant great protection from quelaunts, even after death.

effect->Spell Effect: Fly.name: Spell Effect: Fly
effect->Spell Effect: Fly.description:
Granted by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.A2JfEKe6BZcTG1S8]{Fly}

The target can soar through the air, gaining a fly Speed equal to its Speed or 20 feet, whichever is greater.

action->Emotional Focus.name: Emotional Focus
action->Emotional Focus.description: The quelaunt can cast the following cleric domain spells as 8th-rank occult innate spells at will without spending Focus Points: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.jJphHQlENHFlSElH]{Captivating Adoration}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.J8pL8yTshga8QOk8]{Delusional Pride}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Dj44lViYKvOJ8a53]{Ignite Ambition}.

action->Emotional Frenzy.name: Emotional Frenzy
action->Emotional Frenzy.description: The quelaunt casts up to three spells chosen from its at-will innate spells and its emotional focus spells.

action->Feed on Emotion.name: Feed on Emotion
action->Feed on Emotion.description:
Frequency once per round;

Effect The quelaunt feeds on the emotional unrest of a single creature within 30 feet that's under a harmful emotion effect. The target must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:37] save or take @Damage[4d10[mental]] damage and be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned} for 1 round.

If the target fails its saving throw, the quelaunt regains the same number of Hit Points and regains the action it spent to Feed on Emotion. It can't use the regained action to Feed on Emotion again.

action->Rapid Strikes.name: Rapid Strikes
action->Rapid Strikes.description: The quelaunt makes three melee Strikes, each against a different target within reach. The multiple attack penalty applies to each attack but increases only after all the attacks have been made.

action->Spiral of Despair.name: Spiral of Despair
action->Spiral of Despair.description:
Trigger A creature fails a saving throw to resist one of the quelaunt's innate spells or emotional focus spells

Effect As the quelaunt invades the triggering creature's mind and plants the seeds of negative emotions, it also strips away the target's feelings of hope or positivity. The quelaunt can immediately end a single emotion effect from which the triggering creature is benefiting.

weapon->Bottled Lightning (Greater).name: Bottled Lightning (Greater)
weapon->Bottled Lightning (Greater).description:
Activate A Strike

Bottled lightning is packed with volatile reagents that create a blast of electricity when they are exposed to air. Bottled lightning deals 3d6 electricity damage and 3 electricity splash damage. On a hit, the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of your next turn. You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls.

weapon->Serpent Dagger.name: Serpent Dagger
weapon->Serpent Dagger.description:
The serrated blade of this +1 striking dagger has a greenish tinge, and the hilt is sculpted to look like the head of a serpent about to strike.

When you critically succeed at an attack roll with the serpent dagger, the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} unless it succeeds at a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save. This is a poison effect.

In addition, you can activate the dagger to poison a creature with a more potent poison.

Activate—Drip Poison F (manipulate)

Frequency once per day

Trigger You damage a creature with the serpent dagger

Effect You poison the creature you hit with dagger venom.

Dagger Venom (poison)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:21]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1}

weapon->Acid Flask (Moderate).name: Acid Flask (Moderate)
weapon->Acid Flask (Moderate).description:
Activate A Strike

This flask filled with corrosive acid deals 1 acid damage, 2d6 persistent acid damage, and 2 acid splash damage. You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls.

weapon->Alchemist's Fire (Moderate).name: Alchemist's Fire (Moderate)
weapon->Alchemist's Fire (Moderate).description:
Activate A Strike

Alchemist's fire is a combination of volatile liquids that ignite when exposed to air. Alchemist's fire deals 2d8 fire damage, 2 persistent fire damage, and 2 fire splash damage. You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls.

armor->Hide Armor.name: Hide Armor
armor->Hide Armor.description: A mix of furs, sturdy hide, and sometimes molded boiled leather, this armor provides protection due to its layers of leather, though its bulkiness slows the wearer down and decreases mobility.

equipment->Alchemist Goggles.name: Alchemist Goggles
equipment->Alchemist Goggles.description:
These brass goggles are engraved with flame patterns and have thick, heavy lenses.

While worn, they give you a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.rmwa3OyhTZ2i2AHl]{Craft} alchemical items. When making Strikes with alchemical bombs, you ignore lesser cover.

If your Strike with an alchemical bomb fails (but doesn't critically fail), you gain a +1 item bonus to the splash damage the target of the Strike takes.

equipment->Alchemist's Toolkit.name: Alchemist's Toolkit
equipment->Alchemist's Toolkit.description: This mobile collection of vials and chemicals can be used for simple alchemical tasks. If you wear your alchemist's toolkit, you can draw and replace it as part of the action that uses it.

equipment->Formula Book.name: Formula Book
equipment->Formula Book.description: A formula book holds the formulas necessary to make items other than the common equipment from Pathfinder Player Core; characters of the alchemist class typically get one for free. Each formula book can hold the formulas for up to 100 different items. Formulas can also appear on parchment sheets, tablets, and almost any other medium; there's no need for you to copy them into a specific book as long as you can keep them on hand to reference them.

consumable->Blightburn Resin.name: Blightburn Resin
consumable->Blightburn Resin.description:
This tacky, hardened sap is harvested from trees infected by fungal blights and exposed to open flames.

Activate A (manipulate)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:30]

Onset 1 minute

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[7d6[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[9d6[poison]] damage (1 round)

consumable->Wyvern Poison.name: Wyvern Poison
consumable->Wyvern Poison.description:
Properly harvested and preserved, the poison from a wyvern's sting is effective and direct.

Activate 2 (manipulate)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:26]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[3d8[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d10[poison]] damage (1 round)

action->Alchemical Formulas.name: Alchemical Formulas
action->Alchemical Formulas.description:

action->Drip Poison.name: Drip Poison
action->Drip Poison.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger You damage a creature with the serpent dagger

Effect You poison the creature you hit with dagger venom.

Dagger Venom (poison)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:21]

Maximum Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1}

action->Pinpoint Poisoner.name: Pinpoint Poisoner
action->Pinpoint Poisoner.description: When Racid successfully Strikes an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creature with a poisoned weapon or exposes an off-guard creature to an inhaled poison, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its initial save against the poison.

action->Quick Alchemy.name: Quick Alchemy
action->Quick Alchemy.description: Racid creates a single alchemical item from her formula book (see Alchemical Formulas) or a versatile vial. This item has the infused trait, but it remains potent only until the start of her next turn. With her typical preparations, she can use this ability 7 times per day.

action->Quick Poison.name: Quick Poison
action->Quick Poison.description: Racid draws and applies a dose of poison to a weapon she's holding.

action->Toxic Splash.name: Toxic Splash
action->Toxic Splash.description: Splash damage dealt by Racid's alchemical bombs is increased to 5.

weapon->Boot Knife.name: Boot Knife
weapon->Boot Knife.description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

weapon->Flail.name: Flail
weapon->Flail.description: This weapon consists of a wooden handle attached to a spiked ball or cylinder by a chain, rope, or strap of leather.

armor->Half Plate.name: Half Plate
armor->Half Plate.description: Half plate consists of most of the upper body plates used in full plate, with lighter or sparser steel plate protection for the arms and legs. This provides some of the protection of full plate with greater flexibility and speed. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.zBYEU9E7034ENCmh]{Padded Armor} and a pair of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Ix2vicchE79d6Cl3]{gauntlets}.

consumable->Elixir of Life (Major).name: Elixir of Life (Major)
consumable->Elixir of Life (Major).description:
Activate A (manipulate)

Elixirs of life accelerate a living creature's natural healing processes and immune system. Upon drinking this elixir, you regain @Damage[(8d6+21)[healing]] Hit Points and gain +3 item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.lPRuIRbu0rHBkoKY]{Effect: Elixir of Life}

action->Antimagic Armor.name: Antimagic Armor
action->Antimagic Armor.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger A spell targets you or includes you in its area

Effect The armor attempts to counteract the triggering spell with the effect of a 7th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} spell and a counteract modifier of [[/r 1d20+26 #Counteract]]{+26}.

action->Cushioned Blow.name: Cushioned Blow
action->Cushioned Blow.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger Rayhnne is critically hit with a Strike or spell attack

Effect Rayhnne attempts a @Check[flat|dc:11]. On a success, the attack becomes a normal hit instead.

action->Guardbreaker.name: Guardbreaker
action->Guardbreaker.description: Rayhnne makes a Strike against a creature, then Strides. This Stride doesn't trigger reactions. If she ends this Stride in a difference space adjacent to the same creature, she makes another Strike against it. If both Strikes succeed, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of Rayhnne's next turn. Each attack counts against Rayhnne's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until she's made both attacks.

action->Smashing Wave.name: Smashing Wave
action->Smashing Wave.description: Rayhnne smashes her flails together, creating a localized shock wave. She chooses whether this effect is a @Template[type:emanation|distance:15] or a @Template[type:cone|distance:15]. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[10d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:34|basic] saving throw. A creature that fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round. A creature that critically fails is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 minute.

action->Spinning Shield.name: Spinning Shield
action->Spinning Shield.description:
Rayhnne spins her flails rapidly in all directions, giving her a +2 circumstance bonus to her AC and Reflex saves until the start of her next turn. If she uses Reactive Strike while this ability is active, she adds 1d10 to her damage.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.8hznLlJ0wBazTw8n]{Effect: Spinning Shield}

Red-Tinged Doorway.name: Red-Tinged Doorway
Red-Tinged Doorway.descriptionHazard: A swirling portal leads to unwholesome places.
Red-Tinged Doorway.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:35], @Check[occultism|dc:35], or @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master); must have studied Carlina's notes

action->Warping Journey.name: Warping Journey
action->Warping Journey.description:
Trigger A creature passes through the doorway

Effect The creature is pulled apart and reassembled as they pass through multiple dimensions at once. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save and disappears for a number of rounds, reappearing at the end of their turn on the final round. Each round at the beginning of their turn, creatures trapped in the doorway must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save against a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.dN8QBNuTiaBHCKUe]{Cursed Metamorphosis} spell as their bodies are warped and rearranged. A creature can pass through each red-tinged doorway only once.

Critical Success The creature disappears for 1 round. Additionally, the creature gains 10 temporary Hit Points for 24 hours. These stack with temporary Hit Points gained from other red-tinged doorways and replenish each day at midnight as long as the creature is within 1 mile of Narona.

Success The creature disappears for 1 round.

Failure The creature disappears for 2 rounds.

Critical Failure The creature disappears for 3 rounds. If the creature critically fails its saving throw against a cursed metamorphosis spell, it's permanently transformed into an amalgamite.

Reynard Hawk.name: Reynard Hawk
Reynard Hawk.publicNotes:
Some hunters grow bored of simple beasts and monsters. For them, a battle-tested warrior is the finest prey.

Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

weapon->Greataxe.name: Greataxe
weapon->Greataxe.description: This large battle axe is too heavy to wield with only one hand. Many greataxes incorporate two blades, and they are often "bearded," having a hook at the bottom to increase the strength of their chopping power.

weapon->Hand Crossbow.name: Hand Crossbow
weapon->Hand Crossbow.description: Sometimes referred to as an alley bow by rogues or ruffians, this small crossbow fires small bolts that are sometimes used to deliver poison to the target. It's small enough to be shot one-handed, but it still requires two hands to load.

armor->Studded Leather Armor.name: Studded Leather Armor
armor->Studded Leather Armor.description: This leather armor is reinforced with metal studs and sometimes small metal plates, providing most of the flexibility of leather armor with more robust protection.

equipment->Hellfire Boots.name: Hellfire Boots
equipment->Hellfire Boots.description:
These heavy boots are made of blackened metal and always feel warm to the touch, with streams of glowing embers cascading off their heels. While wearing hellfire boots, you gain resistance 10 to fire damage.

Activate—Devil's Dance 2 (manipulate)

Frequency once per minute

Effect You Stride. Each square you move through during your Stride is scorched with hellish flames, becoming hazardous terrain for 1 minute. A creature that moves through one of these spaces takes @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Prepared Trapper.name: Prepared Trapper
action->Prepared Trapper.description: A hero hunter carries the materials to Craft two @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.AFX1V0Go9DqPWBlN]{Alarm Snares}, two @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.TFnr9Gq7VXPJu0GQ]{Grasping Snares}, one @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.j9G3tnrKQ1N1dLzN]{Snagging Hook Snare}, and one @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.a2XaerM1KkPyLIPM]{Stunning Snare}. The hero hunter replenishes any used supplies each time they make their daily preparations.

action->Nimble Dodge.name: Nimble Dodge
action->Nimble Dodge.description:
Trigger The hero hunter is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker they can see

Effect The hero hunter gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

action->Deadly Snares.name: Deadly Snares
action->Deadly Snares.description: The hero hunter Crafts a snare that would normally take 1 minute or less to Craft. The Stealth DC to locate the snare and DC to disable it with Thievery are equal to the hero hunter's Crafting DC if it's higher than the snare's DC.

action->Devil's Dance.name: Devil's Dance
action->Devil's Dance.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect You Stride. Each square you move through during your Stride is scorched with hellish flames, becoming hazardous terrain for 1 minute. A creature that moves through one of these spaces takes @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage.

action->Felling Shot.name: Felling Shot
action->Felling Shot.description: The hero hunter makes a ranged Strike. If it hits and deals damage to a flying target, the target falls up to 120 feet but takes no damage from the fall. The creature can't Fly, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.d5I6018Mci2SWokk]{Leap}, levitate or otherwise leave the ground until the end of the hero hunter's next turn.

action->Hunter's Precision.name: Hunter's Precision
action->Hunter's Precision.description: The hero hunter knows how to hunt and kill any game. While in this stance, all the hero hunter's Strikes deal an additional 2d8 precision damage, and the range increment for their ranged weapon Strikes is 20 feet longer than normal. If the hunter gets a critical hit with a weapon Strike, the target also takes 2d6 persistent bleed damage.

action->Infernal Jig.name: Infernal Jig
action->Infernal Jig.description: The Sea Mantis is immune to the flames created by Reynard when he activates his hellfire boots.

weapon->General's Word.name: General's Word
weapon->General's Word.description:
The mere act of wielding this heavy +2 greater striking thundering mace grants you a distinct air of authority and gravitas. While wielding the weapon, you can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.W02bHXylIpoXbO4e]{Bullhorn} as a 1st-rank cantrip at will.

Activate—Battlefield Broadcast 2 (concentrate, linguistic, manipulate, mental, occult)

Frequency once per day

Effect The weapon amplifies your force of personality across the battlefield with the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.HqTI6wRrck1YXp3F]{Telepathy} for 10 minutes, except the range is 500 feet. The effect ends immediately if you drop your weapon.

Craft Requirements Supply one casting of bullhorn and telepathy.

armor->Breastplate.name: Breastplate
armor->Breastplate.description: Though referred to as a breastplate, this type of armor consists of several pieces of plate or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.pRoikbRo5HFW6YUB]{Half Plate} armor that protect the torso, chest, neck, and sometimes the hips and lower legs. It strategically grants some of the protection of plate while allowing greater flexibility and speed.

equipment->Religious Symbol of Asmodeus.name: Religious Symbol of Asmodeus
equipment->Religious Symbol of Asmodeus.description: This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol can be worn on the body on a chain or pin, or can be held.

action->Call of Asmodeus.name: Call of Asmodeus
action->Call of Asmodeus.description:
Frequency three times per day

Requirements A troop within sight of Rotrovio loses a segment

Effect Rotrovio reanimates the recently slain to rise and fight again. Rotrovio casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.0JWyMwVnLxX9CDYQ]{Rouse Skeletons} as a 5th-rank innate divine spell (Pathfinder Player Core 2 250), including at least one square the lost troop segment previously occupied in the initial area of effect. The triggering troop gains a +4 circumstance bonus to its saves against this spell.

action->Battlefield Broadcast.name: Battlefield Broadcast
action->Battlefield Broadcast.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The weapon amplifies your force of personality across the battlefield with the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.HqTI6wRrck1YXp3F]{Telepathy} for 10 minutes, except the range is 500 feet. The effect ends immediately if you drop your weapon.

action->Sea Speech.name: Sea Speech
action->Sea Speech.description: A sedacthy speaking Thalassic can be understood by any animal that has a swim Speed or the amphibious or aquatic trait. By spending a week regularly interacting with such an animal, the sedacthy can make it permanently helpful.

action->Frenzied Feast.name: Frenzied Feast
action->Frenzied Feast.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The warband launches a frenzy of attacks against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] with a @Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save. The damage depends on the number of actions. For each enemy damaged by this attack, the damage of the attack increases by 1d4 piercing for all targets (up to a maximum of 3d4 additional damage).

1 @Damage[(1d6+3)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus @Damage[1d4[persistent,bleed]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d6+6)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(3d6+9)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus @Damage[1d8[persistent,bleed]|options:area-damage] damage

action->War Shriek.name: War Shriek
action->War Shriek.description:
The warband unleashes a chorus of terrifying battle cries. Each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must attempt a @Check[will|dc:29] save. Regardless of the results, creatures are temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}.

Sigil of Deepest Fear.name: Sigil of Deepest Fear
Sigil of Deepest Fear.descriptionHazard: An arcane sigil forces creatures to confront their greatest fear.
Sigil of Deepest Fear.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:43] (master) to drain the sigil's power harmlessly, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (9th rank; counteract DC 42) to counteract the sigil

action->Face Your Fear.name: Face Your Fear
action->Face Your Fear.description:
Trigger A creature moves within 10 feet of the sigil

Effect All creatures within 120 feet of the sigil are affected by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.MJx7DmjsWYzDZ3a4]{Phantasmagoria} spell (@Check[will|dc:40] save).

Smoking Chimney.name: Smoking Chimney
Smoking Chimney.descriptionHazard: Smoke billows out from the chimney and fills the room.
Smoking Chimney.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:40] (expert) to pull the blockage from the chimney, @Check[thievery|dc:46] (master) to clear the chimney by removing a crucial object and cause everything to fall out, or create water (or similar effect) to extinguish the fire
Smoking Chimney.reset: The smoking chimney resets when 10 Bulk of objects are placed in it.
Smoking Chimney.routine: (1 action; inhaled, poison) The smoking chimney billows out smoke. If the door from area A6 is open, the smoke expands 5 feet into the corridor. All creatures in the smoke take @Damage[11d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:43|basic|traits:inhaled,poison] save). On a failure, the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} for 1 round.

action->Choking Smoke.name: Choking Smoke
action->Choking Smoke.description:
Trigger A creature other than Ivirno enters area A6

Effect Smoke fills the room, concealing all creatures within area A6. All creatures in the area other than Ivirno take @Damage[11d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:43|basic] save), then the smoking chimney rolls initiative.

Soul-Ripper.name: Soul-Ripper
Soul-Ripper.descriptionHazard: Two hosts of shadowy spirits emerge from Hell to rip Oourax's soul in two, damaging all those who stand in their path.
Soul-Ripper.disable: @Check[diplomacy|dc:33] or @Check[deception|dc:33] to convince the spirits to ignore you, or @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] to weaken the haunt; four successes are required to disable it. After 5 rounds, Oourax's soul is sundered and the haunt ends.
Soul-Ripper.routine: (1 action) Oourax's soul attempts to flee, moving 20 feet in a random direction. Her soul is pursued by two hosts of devilish spirits. Large or smaller creatures (or troops composed of Large or smaller creatures) within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] of Oourax's soul are trampled by the spirits (@Damage[(2d10+13)[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save).

action->Devil's Due.name: Devil's Due
action->Devil's Due.description:
Trigger Oourax dies

Effect Oourax's soul rises from her body. The haunt rolls initiative.

action->Short Salvo.name: Short Salvo
action->Short Salvo.description: The special forces unit draws and fires a barrage of arrows from their shortbows. The salvo is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet that deals @Damage[5d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:34|basic] save). When the special forces unit is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Slashing Blades.name: Slashing Blades
action->Slashing Blades.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The special forces unit attacks each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] with its various blades, with a @Check[reflex|dc:34|basic] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(3d6+2)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(5d6+7)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(8d6+10)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

Steaming Flow.name: Steaming Flow
Steaming Flow.descriptionHazard: The elemental-infused fountain spouts boiling water in a singular direction, carrying all non-aquatic creatures caught in it across the hallway and toward the perilous drop-off in the opposite tower!
Steaming Flow.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:25] (trained), @Check[nature|dc:25] (trained), or @Check[survival|dc:25] (trained) to divert the flow into weaker streams, or @Check[athletics|dc:27], @Check[crafting|dc:27] (trained), or @Check[thievery|dc:27] (trained) to break up the ground to weaken the flow; two successes (or one critical success) are needed to disable the hazard.
Steaming Flow.routine: (1 action) The hazard attempts an Athletics check with a +28 modifier to [[/act shove]] all creatures in the water-filled areas in areas E5 and E6 toward the drop-off in area E7; the boiling spring gains a +4 circumstance bonus to its Fortitude DC against this effect. Any creature ending its turn in the flow also takes @Damage[(2d12+12)[fire]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:29|basic] save.

action->Babbling Brook.name: Babbling Brook
action->Babbling Brook.description:
Trigger The boiling spring rolls initiative

Effect The steaming flow rolls initiative.

Storm-Tossed Sea.name: Storm-Tossed Sea
Storm-Tossed Sea.descriptionHazard: A relentless storm assails the ship, battering those on board who don't secure themselves.
Storm-Tossed Sea.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:29], Crafting, or Sailing Lore to securely lash one's self to the ship. Creatures tied to the ship are immune to the hazard's routine but are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}.
Storm-Tossed Sea.routine:
(1 action) The storm tosses the ship in a random direction (roll 1d8 to determine). All creatures on the ship that aren't lashed to it are moved 10 feet in that direction and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:30] save or Sailing Lore check as the waves pummel them.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and can avoid being moved 10 feet if it wishes.

Success The creature takes no damage.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} and takes @Damage[(1d12+15)[bludgeoning]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} and takes @Damage[(2d12+15)[bludgeoning]] damage.

action->Storm Surge.name: Storm Surge
action->Storm Surge.description:
Trigger The storm builds for at least 10 minutes

Effect The ship pitches dangerously atop the waves. The hazard rolls initiative and performs its routine as a free action.

Stygian Serpent.name: Stygian Serpent
Stygian Serpent.publicNotes:
These fabled beasts resemble massive snakes with long rows of finned spines down their back. Temperamental and territorial, sea serpents can capsize a boat with ease, and most won't hesitate to do so when hungry or threatened. Stories abound of aggrieved captains who spend their entire lives hunting down the elusive monster that sunk their ships and took their livelihoods. These hunts rely on rumors and glimpses of the beasts, as few survive the catastrophes wrought by sea serpents.

While many fishermen's tales paint sea serpents as divinely appointed guardians of the ocean or as evil and demonic agents, the truth is that most sea serpents are simply very large beasts with a knack for avoiding magical detection.

action->Undetectable.name: Undetectable
action->Undetectable.description: A sea serpent automatically tries to counteract any detection, revelation, or scrying divination attempted against it, using its @Check[stealth] modifier for its counteract modifier.

action->Stygian.name: Stygian
action->Stygian.description: The serpent is immune to effects of the River Styx.

action->Capsize.name: Capsize
action->Capsize.description: The sea serpent attempts to capsize an aquatic vessel of its size or smaller that it's adjacent to. It must succeed at an @Check[athletics|dc:35] check with a DC of 35 (reduced by 5 for each size smaller than the sea serpent) or the pilot's Sailing Lore DC, whichever is higher.

action->Sea Serpent Algae.name: Sea Serpent Algae
action->Sea Serpent Algae.description:
The water in the ballast organs around the sea serpent's neck is full of psychotropic algae.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:34]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} and, if flying, spends its first action each turn to descend 20 feet (1 round)

Stage 2 confused and, if flying, descends until reaching the ground or water below (1 round)

action->Spine Rake.name: Spine Rake
action->Spine Rake.description: The sea serpent extends the spines along its back and Swims or Strides. Each creature the serpent is adjacent to at any point during its movement takes @Damage[(4d6+8)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:32|basic] save).

weapon->Spear.name: Spear
weapon->Spear.description: A long metal shaft ending with a metal spike, a spear can be used one-handed as a melee weapon and can be thrown.

action->Captor's Bane.name: Captor's Bane
action->Captor's Bane.description: Talmandor's Strikes deal an additional @Damage[2d8[spirit]] damage against any creature that has hindered another creature—by imposing a penalty to a creature's Speed or imposing the clumsy, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} conditions (including using an action to extend the duration of a grab) to another creature—since the beginning of Talmandor's last turn. One ally hindered by the target can attempt to break free of one of the hindering effects; they can either attempt a new save against the effect that allows a save or attempt to Escape from the effect as a free action.

action->Coruscating Charge.name: Coruscating Charge
action->Coruscating Charge.description:
Talmandor briefly transforms into a luminous cloud, which doubles his resistance to physical damage, grants him immunity to precision damage, and allows him to move through most creatures and tiny cracks. He then Flies his Speed before returning to his solid form.

A burst of searing radiance surrounds Talmandor in a @Template[type:burst|distance:20] either before or after he becomes a cloud. Each enemy in the area must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:46] save or take @Damage[2d8[persistent,spirit]|options:area-damage] damage and be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} until the persistent spirit damage ends. Unholy creatures that fail their save are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round, after which they're dazzled for the persistent damage's duration.

Once Talmandor uses this ability, he can't do so again for [[/gmr 1d6 #Recharge do so]]{1d6 rounds}.

action->Twin-Talon Swipe.name: Twin-Talon Swipe
action->Twin-Talon Swipe.description: Talmandor makes two claw Strikes, targeting either the same foe or two adjacent targets. Each attack uses Talmandor's current multiple attack penalty. If they both hit the same target, he combines both Strikes' damage and applies resistances and weaknesses only once.

action->Zephyr Strikes.name: Zephyr Strikes
action->Zephyr Strikes.description: When Talmandor scores a critical hit with a weapon or unarmed Strike, he can Fly or Stride up to 10 feet without triggering reactions.

Tareth Ivici.name: Tareth Ivici
Tareth Ivici.publicNotes:
The infernal registrar is a mortal representative of Hell's bureaucracy. They can access copies of all infernal contracts signed by a denizen of their world and can navigate the machinations of Hell's many devils. They can grant special access—for a price.

Larger societies rely on those with the authority and the ability to interpret and enforce laws. Some carry out these duties fairly, but others are harsh and cruel, imposing severe punishments on anyone unable to pay for clemency.
Tareth Ivici.allSavesBonus: +2 circumstance to all saves vs. fear

weapon->Staff of Office.name: Staff of Office
weapon->Staff of Office.description: This long piece of wood can aid in walking and deliver a mighty blow.

equipment->Masquerade Scarf (Greater).name: Masquerade Scarf (Greater)
equipment->Masquerade Scarf (Greater).description:
This delicately embroidered scarf matches with every outfit and can even complete a costume or disguise with illusions.

Activate—Masquerade 2 (manipulate)

Effect You arrange the scarf over your lower face, and it casts a 2nd-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.i35dpZFI7jZcRoBo]{Illusory Disguise} spell on you, which ends immediately if the scarf is removed. You can alter the scarf's appearance or make it invisible as part of the illusory disguise, but it can still be felt if touched.

consumable->Wand of Alarm (Rank 3).name: Wand of Alarm (Rank 3)
consumable->Wand of Alarm (Rank 3).description:
@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4WAib3GichxLjp5p]{Alarm}

This baton is about a foot long and contains a single spell. The appearance typically relates to the spell within.

Activate Cast a Spell

Frequency once per day, plus overcharge

Effect You Cast the Spell at the indicated rank.

Craft Requirements Supply a casting of the spell at the listed rank.

Note: To create a scroll or wand of a specific spell, drag the spell from the compendium or compendium browser into the inventory of a PC, NPC, or loot actor.

action->Contract Specialist.name: Contract Specialist
action->Contract Specialist.description: For encounters involving matters of contracts and dealings with Hell, the infernal registrar is an 18th-level challenge.

action->Death is a Promotion.name: Death is a Promotion
action->Death is a Promotion.description: The infernal registrar does not fear death, as they have a signed infernal contract for immediate promotion to a mid-ranked devil upon their soul's arrival in Hell. They're immune to all Intimidation checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.tHCqgwjtQtzNqVvd]{Coerce} involving threats of death.

action->Friends in Low Places.name: Friends in Low Places
action->Friends in Low Places.description: Though devils do not respect most mortals, they respect the office of infernal registrar. No creature with the devil trait of 18th level or lower will knowingly and willingly attack an infernal registrar.

action->Technically Correct.name: Technically Correct
action->Technically Correct.description: The infernal registrar uses their Legal Lore modifier on all Deception checks to [[/act lie statistic=legal-lore]]{Lie}.

action->+2 Circumstance to All Saves vs..name: +2 Circumstance to All Saves vs. Fear

action->Masquerade.name: Masquerade
action->Masquerade.description: You arrange the scarf over your lower face, and it casts a 2nd-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.i35dpZFI7jZcRoBo]{Illusory Disguise} spell on you, which ends immediately if the scarf is removed. You can alter the scarf's appearance or make it invisible as part of the illusory disguise, but it can still be felt if touched.

action->A Favor for a Favor.name: A Favor for a Favor
action->A Favor for a Favor.description: The infernal registrar has the authority to make limited infernal contracts with other mortals. They summon a contract with the legal language they desire. Detecting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} clauses in the contract requires a successful @Check[society|dc:43] or a @Check[legal-lore|dc:38] check. Once signed, the contract vanishes into the infernal filing system in Hell. The infernal registrar cannot grant any boons beyond their own personal power (usually limited to information, advice, or access to elements of the infernal bureaucracy).

action->Request Document.name: Request Document
action->Request Document.description:
The infernal registrar makes a request to summon a copy of any infernal contract a specific creature has signed. They must know enough information to specifically identify the individual who signed. The infernal registrar attempts a @Check[legal-lore] check with a DC equal to a hard DC of the level of the creature in question. The infernal registrar will never promise a successful use of this ability in the agreements they make. Each agreement is typically for one attempt. Any copy summoned is simply a copy, has no impact on the original contract if destroyed or altered, and will vanish if taken more than 20 feet from the infernal registrar.

Critical Success A copy of the contract appears before the infernal registrar after 10 minutes.

Success A copy of the contract appears before the infernal registrar after 1 hour.

Failure The attempt fails, but the infernal registrar can try again after 24 hours.

Critical Failure The attempt fails, and the infernal registrar can't try again for the named creature for 1 year.

weapon->Composite Shortbow.name: Composite Shortbow
weapon->Composite Shortbow.description: This shortbow is made from horn, wood, and sinew laminated together to increase the power of its pull and the force of its projectile. Its compact size and power make it a favorite of mounted archers. Any time an ability is specifically restricted to a shortbow, it also applies to composite shortbows unless otherwise stated.

weapon->War Flail.name: War Flail
weapon->War Flail.description: This large flail has a long shaft connected to a shorter piece of stout wood or metal that's sometimes inlaid with spikes.

armor->Full Plate.name: Full Plate
armor->Full Plate.description: Plate mail consists of interlocking plates that encase nearly the entire body in a carapace of steel. It is costly and heavy, and the wearer often requires help to don it correctly, but it provides some of the best defense armor can supply. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.zBYEU9E7034ENCmh]{Padded Armor} and a pair of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Ix2vicchE79d6Cl3]{gauntlets}.

ammo->Arrows.name: Arrows
ammo->Arrows.description: These projectiles are the ammunition for bows. The shaft of an arrow is made of wood. It is stabilized in flight by fletching at one end and bears a metal head on the other.

action->Rejuvenation.name: Rejuvenation
action->Rejuvenation.description:
When a graveknight is destroyed, their armor rebuilds their body over the course of [[/gmr 1d10]] days—or more quickly if the armor is worn by a living host. If the body is destroyed before then, the process restarts.

A graveknight can only be permanently destroyed by obliterating their armor (such as with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.r7ihOgKv19eJQnik]{Disintegrate}), transporting it to the Forge of Creation, or throwing it into the heart of a volcano.

action->Sacrilegious Aura.name: Sacrilegious Aura
action->Sacrilegious Aura.description:
30 feet.

When a creature in the aura uses a vitality spell or ability, the graveknight automatically attempts to counteract it, with a counteract modifier of [[/r 1d20+0 #Counteract]]{+22}.

action->Thorned Armor.name: Thorned Armor
action->Thorned Armor.description:
Trigger A creature grapples the Foresworn or Strikes him with an unarmed or melee weapon without the reach trait

Effect Barbs tangle the triggering creature's attacking limb, and it must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:30] save. On a failure, it must attempt a @Check[flat|dc:5] to move away from the Foresworn. On a critical failure, this flat check increases to DC 10.

action->Devastating Blast.name: Devastating Blast
action->Devastating Blast.description:
The graveknight unleashes a @Template[cone|distance:30] of energy. Creatures in the area take @Damage[20d4[electricity]|traits:area-damage] for a creature of the graveknight's level with a @Check[reflex|dc:33|basic|options:area-effect].

The graveknight can use this ability once every [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Devastating Blast]] rounds.

action->Graveknight's Curse.name: Graveknight's Curse
action->Graveknight's Curse.description:
This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour

Saving Throw @Check[will|dc:37] save

Onset 1 hour

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} and can't remove armor (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 2}, –10-foot status penalty to Speeds, and can't remove armor (1 day)

Stage 3 dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight.

action->Phantom Mount.name: Phantom Mount
action->Phantom Mount.description:
HP 78; AC 30, Fort +22, Ref +16, Will +19

The graveknight summons a supernatural mount, as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.WPKJOhEihhcIm2uQ]{Marvelous Mount} heightened to a rank equal to half the graveknight's level. Unlike marvelous mount, the steed's AC and saving throw bonuses are all 4 lower than the graveknight's, and the steed has one-third the graveknight's Hit Points (rounded down).

If the steed is destroyed, the graveknight must wait 1 hour before using this ability again.

action->Weapon Master.name: Weapon Master
action->Weapon Master.description: The graveknight has access to the critical specialization effects of any weapons it wields.

Thephrol.name: Thephrol
Thephrol.publicNotes:
Gigantic loners, the great cyclopes are their lesser kin writ large. They're stronger and more violent, but their uncontrolled vision of possible futures has driven them beyond reason. They see every moment as a potential storm of uncontrollable fury and, out of a desperate desire for peace, quiet, and an end to their hunger, lash out at any who come near. Wise creatures avoid great cyclopes at all cost. It's fortunate for humanoids that great cyclopes prefer to dwell far from settlements.

Debate has long raged over the origins of these massive, destructive giants. They are so large that it had long been assumed they were used as beasts of burden by their lesser kin, now free to hunt and kill without restraint. Other scholars believe the great cyclops is the ultimate fate of the entire cyclops species. Whatever foolish decision or wayward curse caused the end of their civilization is still playing out, occasionally causing a cyclops to withdraw from their own kind, lose all semblance of intellect, and mutate into a lumbering, feral colossus.

The cyclopes are violent giants with a tragic past. Although they possess only one eye, they could once see far more than most, possessing occult wisdom and divinatory magic that gave them the mystic ability of foresight. But their legendary oracular powers failed to prevent the fall of their society, and the vast kingdoms of the cyclops long ago collapsed into ruin. Today, cyclopes have forgotten much of what they once knew, and they skulk among the crumbling remains of their fallen cities like forgotten kings and queens of their own fallen kingdoms.

weapon->Greatclub.name: Greatclub
weapon->Greatclub.description: While many greatclubs are intricately carved, others are little more than a sturdy tree branch. These massive clubs are too heavy to wield with only one hand.

armor->Hide Armor.name: Hide Armor
armor->Hide Armor.description: A mix of furs, sturdy hide, and sometimes molded boiled leather, this armor provides protection due to its layers of leather, though its bulkiness slows the wearer down and decreases mobility.

action->Displace.name: Displace
action->Displace.description:
Trigger The amalgamite is hit by a Strike

Effect The amalgamite Repositions itself or Transposes itself with another creature. If the amalgamite successfully Transposes its location, it and any transposed creature take half damage from the triggering Strike.

action->Flash of Brutality.name: Flash of Brutality
action->Flash of Brutality.description:
Frequency once per day, and recharges when the great cyclops uses Ferocity.

Trigger The great cyclops succeeds at an attack roll.

Effect The attack becomes a critical success.

action->Powerful Charge.name: Powerful Charge
action->Powerful Charge.description: The great cyclops Strides twice and makes a horn Strike. If it moved at least 20 feet away from its starting position, the Strike's damage is increased to 3d10+20.

action->Reposition.name: Reposition
action->Reposition.description: The amalgamite teleports into an unoccupied space it can see within 50 feet.

action->Transpose.name: Transpose
action->Transpose.description: The amalgamite chooses up to two destabilized creatures it can see within 50 feet and swaps their positions, or moves one such creature to an unoccupied space it can see within range. If the amalgamite chooses two creatures, it can choose itself as a target, but both targets must be the same size. An unwilling creature can attempt a @Check[will|dc:33] save. If either target succeeds at this saving throw, both are unaffected.

Thin Wisps Fireworks Show.name: Thin Wisps Fireworks Show
Thin Wisps Fireworks Show.descriptionHazard: Fashionable performers and revolutionaries rappel from the surrounding buildings on scarves.
Thin Wisps Fireworks Show.disable: @Check[acrobatics|dc:21] to turn away from the explosions, or @Check[crafting|dc:25] to identify the exact moment the fireworks will explode
Thin Wisps Fireworks Show.routine: (1 action; visual) The Thin Wisps continue to launch fireworks until the dottari excruciator divisions are defeated. Each round, all creatures must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:24|traits:visual] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 round (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} and deafened for 1 round on a critical failure).

action->Let the Sparks Fly.name: Let the Sparks Fly
action->Let the Sparks Fly.description:
Trigger A non-dottari creature takes damage

Effect Thin Wisps fire off a series of fireworks as they rappel from the eaves on scarves. All creatures in area A must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save. On a failure, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} until the end of its next turn (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} and deafened on a critical failure). The hazard then rolls initiative.

Threshold Kerulkagon.name: Threshold Kerulkagon
Threshold Kerulkagon.publicNotes: Kerulkagons are elite, griffon-like guardians specially chosen and transformed by the archdevil Barbatos to interrogate anyone using his plane's myriad portals.
Threshold Kerulkagon.allSavesBonus: +2 status to all saves vs. magic

action->+2 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +2 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Anchored Form.name: Anchored Form
action->Anchored Form.description: A kerulkagon can't be teleported against its will.

action->Stand Still.name: Stand Still
action->Stand Still.description:
Trigger A creature within the kerulkagon's reach uses a move action or leaves a square during a move action it's using

Effect The kerulkagon makes a melee Strike against the triggering creature. If the attack is a critical hit, the action is disrupted.

action->Force an Opening.name: Force an Opening
action->Force an Opening.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The kerulkagon creates a momentary portal next to a creature within 30 feet and makes a melee Strike through the portal as if the target were within its reach. The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} and gains no cover against the Strike.

action->Personal Portal.name: Personal Portal
action->Personal Portal.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The kerulkagon teleports to an unoccupied space it can see within 30 feet.

action->Portal Throw.name: Portal Throw
action->Portal Throw.description:
Requirements The kerulkagon has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}

Effect The kerulkagon creates a portal and attempts to throw the required creature through it. The kerulkagon attempts an @Check[athletics|against:fortitude] check against the Fortitude DC of the creature it has grabbed or restrained.

Critical Success The kerulkagon hurls the target through the portal. The target is teleported violently to a location with 100 feet that the kerulkagon can see, dealing @Damage[12d10[bludgeoning]] damage to the target and knocking it @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} on impact.

Success As critical success, but the target takes only @Damage[6d10[bludgeoning]] damage.

Failure The kerulkagon fails to throw the creature.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is no longer grabbed or restrained.

weapon->Dagger.name: Dagger
weapon->Dagger.description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

weapon->Rapier.name: Rapier
weapon->Rapier.description: The rapier is a long and thin piercing blade with a basket hilt. It is prized among many as a dueling weapon.

armor->Leather Armor.name: Leather Armor
armor->Leather Armor.description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

action->Clever Gambit.name: Clever Gambit
action->Clever Gambit.description:
Trigger The agent critically hits a creature that they've identified with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge}

Effect The agent Steps or Strides. This movement doesn't trigger reactions from the triggering creature.

action->Consider Options.name: Consider Options
action->Consider Options.description: The agent Recalls Knowledge about a creature they can see. On a success, the subject is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the agent until the end of the agent's next turn (or for 1 minute on a critical success).

action->Debilitating Strike.name: Debilitating Strike
action->Debilitating Strike.description:

Effect Until the end of the agent's next turn, the target takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds or becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1}. The agent decides which penalty to apply.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.yBTASi3FvnReAwHy]{Effect: Debilitating Strike}

action->Deny Advantage.name: Deny Advantage
action->Deny Advantage.description: The agent isn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to hidden, undetected, or flanking creatures of their level or lower, or creatures their level or lower using surprise attack.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The agent deals an additional 3d6 precision damage to creatures that are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to them.

action->Surprise Attack.name: Surprise Attack
action->Surprise Attack.description: If the agent rolled Deception or Stealth for initiative, creatures that haven’t yet acted are off-guard to them.

Thrune Assassin.name: Thrune Assassin
Thrune Assassin.publicNotes:
Assassins commit murder, either for pay or due to their belief in a cause, such as a religion or a political movement. Many are members of assassins' guilds: organizations that accept contracts to kill in return for money, favors, or both.

In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.
