weapon->Composite Shortbow.description: This shortbow is made from horn, wood, and sinew laminated together to increase the power of its pull and the force of its projectile. Its compact size and power make it a favorite of mounted archers. Any time an ability is specifically restricted to a shortbow, it also applies to composite shortbows unless otherwise stated.

weapon->Rapier.name: Rapier
weapon->Rapier.description: The rapier is a long and thin piercing blade with a basket hilt. It is prized among many as a dueling weapon.

armor->Leather Armor.name: Leather Armor
armor->Leather Armor.description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

consumable->Darkvision Elixir (Lesser).name: Darkvision Elixir (Lesser)
consumable->Darkvision Elixir (Lesser).description:
Activate 1 (manipulate)

After you drink this elixir, your sight becomes sharper in darkness. You gain darkvision for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.7UL8belWmo7U5YGM]{Effect: Darkvision Elixir (Lesser)}

consumable->Giant Centipede Venom.name: Giant Centipede Venom
consumable->Giant Centipede Venom.description:
Giant centipede venom causes severe muscle stiffness.

Activate 2 (manipulate)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:17]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} (1 round)

consumable->Invisibility Potion.name: Invisibility Potion
consumable->Invisibility Potion.description:
Activate A (manipulate)

An invisibility potion is colorless and oddly lightweight. Upon drinking it, you gain the effects of a 2nd-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.XXqE1eY3w3z6xJCB]{Invisibility} spell.

consumable->Lethargy Poison.name: Lethargy Poison
consumable->Lethargy Poison.description:
Lethargy poison is commonly used in hit-and-run tactics by attackers who want their victims alive; the ambusher retreats until the poison sets in and the victim falls unconscious. Further exposure to lethargy poison does not require the target to attempt additional saving throws; only failing an saving throw against an ongoing exposure can progress its stage.

Activate 2 (manipulate)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:18]

Maximum Duration 4 hours

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (1 minute)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} with no Perception check to wake up (1 round)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} with no Perception check to wake up ([[/gmr 1d4 #hours]]{1d4 hours}).

ammo->Arrows.name: Arrows
ammo->Arrows.description: These projectiles are the ammunition for bows. The shaft of an arrow is made of wood. It is stabilized in flight by fletching at one end and bears a metal head on the other.

ammo->Black Tendril Shot (Lesser).name: Black Tendril Shot (Lesser)
ammo->Black Tendril Shot (Lesser).description:
Ammunition any

Activate 1 (concentrate)

A glistening, tar-like substance that's displeasing to the touch coats a black tendril shot. When the activated ammunition hits a target, it exudes tendrils that encase the target, which must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] saving throw. The ammunition's effects last until the target Escapes.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} but gains a +2 circumstance bonus to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} the ammunition's effect.

Failure The target is slowed 1.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2}.

action->Swift Sneak.name: Swift Sneak
action->Swift Sneak.description: The assassin can move their full speed when @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{Sneaking}.

action->Deny Advantage.name: Deny Advantage
action->Deny Advantage.description: The assassin isn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to creatures of 8th level or lower that are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected}, flanking or using @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.classfeatures.Item.w6rMqmGzhUahdnA7]{Surprise Attack}.

action->Nimble Dodge.name: Nimble Dodge
action->Nimble Dodge.description:
Trigger The assassin is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker they can see.

Effect The assassin gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

action->Assassin's Poison.name: Assassin's Poison
action->Assassin's Poison.description:
Requirements The assassin is wielding a piercing or slashing weapon and has a free hand

Effect The assassin applies a poison to the weapon. That poison's DC is increased to 26 if it was lower.

action->Marked for Death.name: Marked for Death
action->Marked for Death.description: Each assassin is armed with one lesser black tendril shot that's engraved with the name of a specific PC. If this arrow successfully strikes that PC, the arrow's saving throw and Escape DCs increase by 5, and the save DC against any poison applied to the arrow by their Assassin's Poison ability increases to DC 30

action->Quick Draw.name: Quick Draw
action->Quick Draw.description: The assassin @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.pvQ5rY2zrtPI614F]{Interacts} to draw a weapon, then @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.VjxZFuUXrCU94MWR]{Strikes} with that weapon.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The assassin deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Surprise Attack.name: Surprise Attack
action->Surprise Attack.description: On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the assassin.

Torment Binder.name: Torment Binder
Torment Binder.publicNotes:
Ghosts and other spirits swirl around the spirit binder, creating a constant aura of flickering faces and forms.

Hidden secrets and occult powers have an irresistible lure for many. Since the majority of these NPCs are spellcasters, consider using alternative spell lists to adjust their themes.

spell->Interplanar Teleport (to or from.name: Interplanar Teleport (to or from the Ethereal Plane only)

action->Spiritsense.name: Spiritsense
action->Spiritsense.description: The spirit binder can sense the spirits of creatures, including living creatures, most non-mindless undead, and haunts within the listed range. Since spiritsense detects spiritual essence, not physical bodies, it can detect spirits projected by spells (such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.0873MWM0qKDDv81O]{Project Image}) or possessing otherwise soulless objects. It can't detect soulless bodies, constructs, or objects, and like most senses, it doesn't penetrate through solid objects.

action->Haunting Spirits.name: Haunting Spirits
action->Haunting Spirits.description: 30 feet. The spirits bound by a spirit binder swirl around, lashing out at their foes. An enemy that enters or starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:27|options:area-effect,damaging-effect,inflicts:frightened] save or take @Damage[3d6[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage and be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (double damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

action->Spirit Scrying.name: Spirit Scrying
action->Spirit Scrying.description: The spirit binder's scrying spells can target or detect spirits on other planes as though the spirits were in the Universe.

action->Succumb to the Void.name: Succumb to the Void
action->Succumb to the Void.description: The spirit binder taps into the more nefarious spirits of the Void, becoming something morbid and cruel. For [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}, their resistance, aura of spirits, Strikes, and spirit spells change their damage type from spirit damage to void damage and replace their spirit trait with the void trait.

Ulat-Kini Kidnappers.name: Ulat-Kini Kidnappers
Ulat-Kini Kidnappers.publicNotes:
Some bands of experienced soldiers have storied reputations, their rolls filled with legendary warriors. Others are whispered of for their cruel deeds and opportunistic betrayals.

Whether they're hired to wage war, protect a caravan, or infiltrate an impenetrable fortress, there's ample work for mercenaries all over Golarion.

action->Troop Movement.name: Troop Movement
action->Troop Movement.description: Whenever a troop moves, you move one of its segments and the other segments follow behind it. At the end of the movement, you can group the other segments adjacent to the one you moved as you see fit, provided none of them moves farther than the moving segment. If you choose not to move the troop any distance, you can instead reshape the position of all the segments as long as one stays in place.

action->Claw and Trident.name: Claw and Trident
action->Claw and Trident.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The ulat-kini kidnappers engages in a coordinated attack with its wide array of melee weapons against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] with a @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d8+2)[piercing]|options:area-damage], or @Damage[(1d8+2)[slashing]|options:area-damage]{slashing}

2 @Damage[(3d8+4)[piercing]|options:area-damage], or @Damage[(3d8+4)[slashing]|options:area-damage]{slashing}

3 @Damage[(4d8+6)[piercing]|options:area-damage], or @Damage[(4d8+6)[slashing]|options:area-damage]{slashing}

action->Ready... Fire!.name: Ready... Fire!
action->Ready... Fire!.description: The ulat-kini kidnappers draws or reloads their bows, crossbows, and slings, then launches a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 120 feet that deals @Damage[(2d8+4)[piercing]|options:area-damage] or @Damage[(2d8+4)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage]{bludgeoning} damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save. When the ulat-kini kidnappers is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Spoils of War.name: Spoils of War
action->Spoils of War.description:
Requirements The kidnappers' last action was Let 'em Have It and at least one creature failed its save

Effect The ulat-kini kidnappers attempts to [[/act steal]] one object from each enemy that failed its save, even if the enemy is in combat or on guard.

weapon->Ranseur.name: Ranseur
weapon->Ranseur.description: This polearm is a long trident with a central prong that's longer than the other two.

action->Sea-Beast Rider.name: Sea-Beast Rider
action->Sea-Beast Rider.description: The ulat-kini scout automatically succeeds at checks to Command an Animal with the aquatic trait that their riding. The first attack the ulat-kini makes each round doesn't share a multiple attack penalty with their mount.

action->Blood in the Water.name: Blood in the Water
action->Blood in the Water.description:
Requirements The ulat-kini scout begins their turn within 30 feet of a creature taking persistent bleed damage

Effect Until the beginning of their next turn, the ulat-kini gains 9 temporary Hit Points and a +4 status bonus to their Strikes' damage rolls.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.gnD8X7SpMaHet941]{Effect: Blood in the Water}

weapon->Handwraps of Mighty Blows.name: Handwraps of Mighty Blows
weapon->Handwraps of Mighty Blows.description:
As you invest these embroidered strips of cloth, you must meditate and slowly wrap them around your hands. These handwraps have weapon runes etched into them to give your unarmed attacks the benefits of those runes, making your unarmed attacks work like magic weapons. For example, +1 striking handwraps of mighty blows would give you a +1 item bonus to attack rolls with your unarmed attacks and increase the damage of your unarmed attacks from one weapon die to two (normally 2d4 instead of 1d4, but if your fists have a different weapon damage die or you have other unarmed attacks, use two of that die size instead).

You can upgrade, add, and transfer runes to and from the handwraps just as you would for a weapon, and you can attach talismans to the handwraps. Treat the handwraps as melee weapons of the brawling group with light Bulk for these purposes. Property runes apply only when they would be applicable to the unarmed attack you're using. For example, a property that must be applied to a slashing weapon wouldn't function when you attacked with a fist, but you would gain its benefits if you attacked with a claw or some other slashing unarmed attack.

weapon->Sling.name: Sling
weapon->Sling.description: Little more than a leather cup attached to a pair of straps, a sling can be used to fling smooth stones or sling bullets at a range.

consumable->Cheap Beer.name: Cheap Beer
consumable->Cheap Beer.description:
Alcohol is a common substance available in stunning variety. The Price of a dose of alcohol depends on the specific beverage. You can't recover from the sickened condition from alcohol while affected.

If you fail a saving throw against alcohol other than initial saves, the affliction's stage doesn't increase; the only way to increase the stage of alcohol's affliction is to consume more alcohol.

Activate A Interact (Ingested)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:12]

Onset 10 minutes

Stage 1 +1 item bonus to saving throws against fear (10 minutes)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard}, +1 item bonus to saving throws against fear effects (10 minutes)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1}, off-guard, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} (10 minutes)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} (10 minutes)

Stage 5 clumsy 2, sickened 2, and stupefied 2 (10 minutes)

Stage 6 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} (8 hours)

Stage 7 death

consumable->Darkvision Elixir (Moderate).name: Darkvision Elixir (Moderate)
consumable->Darkvision Elixir (Moderate).description:
Activate 1 (manipulate)

After you drink this elixir, your sight becomes sharper in darkness. You gain darkvision for 1 hour.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.bcxVvIbuZWOvsKcA]{Effect: Darkvision Elixir (Moderate)}

consumable->Healing Potion (Moderate).name: Healing Potion (Moderate)
consumable->Healing Potion (Moderate).description:
Activate A (manipulate)

A healing potion is a vial of a ruby-red liquid that imparts a tingling sensation as the drinker's wounds heal rapidly. When you drink a healing potion, you regain @Damage[(3d8+10)[healing]] Hit Points.

consumable->Mistform Elixir (Moderate).name: Mistform Elixir (Moderate)
consumable->Mistform Elixir (Moderate).description:
Activate A (manipulate)

A faint mist emanates from your skin, making you @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} for 1 minute.

As usual being concealed when your position is still obvious, you can't use this concealment to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.XMcnh4cSI32tljXa]{Hide} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{Sneak}.

ammo->Sling Bullets.name: Sling Bullets
ammo->Sling Bullets.description: These are small metal balls, typically either iron or lead, designed to be used as ammunition in slings.

ammo->Freezing Sling Bullets.name: Freezing Sling Bullets
ammo->Freezing Sling Bullets.description:
Activate 1 (manipulate)

This chilly ammunition is dark blue and cold to the touch. When activated freezing ammunition hits a target, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round by the intense cold (slowed 1 for 1 minute on a critical failure).

action->Deep Cover Specialist.name: Deep Cover Specialist
action->Deep Cover Specialist.description: For matters relating to spycraft and undercover operations, Urdun Gravelhands is a 14th-level challenge.

action->Favored City.name: Favored City
action->Favored City.description: Urdun can establish a settlement of at least 5,000 people as his favored city by spending 1 week exploring and making contacts there. He can use his Favored City Lore skill to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about the city; he gains a +4 circumstance bonus to his Deception and Stealth checks while in his favored city; and he applies abilities like city shepherd to his allies while in his favored city. Urdun can have only one favored city at a time (Corentyn, at the beginning of this adventure), replacing his prior settlement whenever he establishes a new favored city.

action->+2 Status to All Saves vs. Poiso.name: +2 Status to All Saves vs. Poison

action->City Shepherd.name: City Shepherd
action->City Shepherd.description: While in his favored city, all allies who can observe Urdun can add their level + 2 as their proficiency bonus when attempting Deception or Stealth checks, as if Following the Expert.

action->Fighting Drink.name: Fighting Drink
action->Fighting Drink.description:
Trigger Urdun rolls Initiative

Effect He consumes a dose of alcohol and begins his Soused Ruse as a free action. He rolls Deception for Initiative. Creatures that haven't acted yet are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to him.

action->Soused Ruse.name: Soused Ruse
action->Soused Ruse.description:
Trigger Urdun consumes a dose of alcohol

Effect He appears to become drunk (the same as succumbing to stage 1 of alcohol's poison) without suffering from the condition, and he gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Deception checks to [[/act feint]] against creatures that assume he's intoxicated. A creature can identify his ruse either by Sensing Motive or by succeeding at a Medicine check against his Deception DC while observing him within 30 feet.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Z0aDpqGq715rZXJg]{Effect: Soused Ruse}

action->Break Bottle.name: Break Bottle
action->Break Bottle.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect Urdun shatters a bottle and uses it to brawl. For the next 1 minute when he makes a fist Strike, he can choose for the Strike to lose the unarmed trait, gain the versatile P trait, and deal @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage on critical hits.

action->Ruse Drinker.name: Ruse Drinker
action->Ruse Drinker.description: Urdun Draws and Activates a consumable item he can drink, such as a potion, elixir, or alcoholic drink.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: Urdun's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage against @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Sobering Stride.name: Sobering Stride
action->Sobering Stride.description:
Requirements Urdun's Soused Ruse is active

Effect Urdun pretends to sober up as he moves more quickly. He Strides twice and ends his Soused Ruse.

action->Soused Blow.name: Soused Blow
action->Soused Blow.description:
Requirements Urdun's Soused Ruse is active

Effect Urdun pretends to stumble while preparing a punch. He Feints then makes a melee Strike.

weapon->Longsword.name: Longsword
weapon->Longsword.description: Longswords, also known as arming swords, can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

shield->Sturdy Shield (Lesser).name: Sturdy Shield (Lesser)
shield->Sturdy Shield (Lesser).description:
With a superior design and excellent craftsmanship, this steel shield has higher Hardness than its non-magical counterparts, making it harder to break and destroy.

The shield has Hardness 10, HP 80, and BT 40.

armor->Hellknight Plate.name: Hellknight Plate
armor->Hellknight Plate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight half plate is the armor of choice for Hellknight signifiers, and Hellknight breastplate serves those in the order who lack the training to wear heavy armor.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

equipment->Manacles (Good).name: Manacles (Good)
equipment->Manacles (Good).description: You can manacle someone who is willing or otherwise at your mercy as an exploration activity taking 10-30 seconds depending on the creature's size and how many manacles you apply. A two-legged creature with its legs bound takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds, and a two-handed creature with its wrists bound has to succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] any time it uses a manipulate action or else that action fails. This DC may be higher depending on how tightly the manacles constrain the hands. A creature bound to a stationary object is immobilized. For creatures with more or fewer limbs, the GM determines what effect manacles have, if any. Freeing a creature from good manacles requires five successful @Check[thievery|dc:32|traits:action:pick-a-lock] checks.

action->Hell-Bound Guard.name: Hell-Bound Guard
action->Hell-Bound Guard.description:
Trigger Uro rolls initiative

Effect His hell-bound guard (an elite warden devil) appears and fights to protect him.

action->Chain Choke.name: Chain Choke
action->Chain Choke.description:
Requirements Uro has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}

Effect Uro uses a length of chain to choke his target. The target takes @Damage[(2d6+11)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:29|basic] save). On a failure, the target must begin holding its breath. On a critical failure, the target falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} and starts suffocating.

action->Manacled Grab.name: Manacled Grab
action->Manacled Grab.description: When Uro critically hits with a longsword Strike, he can apply manacles as part of his Improved Grab. If a creature would fail an attempt to Escape his Grab, it critically fails instead.

action->Shield Grab.name: Shield Grab
action->Shield Grab.description: Uro can use his hand wielding a shield to Grab a creature, though he can't Raise a Shield while grabbing or restraining a creature with his shield arm.

Vicious Levaloch Squad.name: Vicious Levaloch Squad
Vicious Levaloch Squad.sensesDetails: (commanded by weak deimavigga or Aspexia Rugatonn for initiative)

action->Hellstrider.name: Hellstrider
action->Hellstrider.description: The levaloch battalion ignores the effects of non-magical difficult terrain. They take no damage from caltrops or from damaging terrain that deals physical, acid, or cold damage. A levaloch battalion can move through liquids up to 5 feet deep at their full Speed.

action->Barbed Net Barrage.name: Barbed Net Barrage
action->Barbed Net Barrage.description:
The battalion hurls barbed nets. This barrage is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 30 feet that deals @Damage[6d8[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:37|traits:magical,unholy] save. A creature can [[/act escape dc=37]] from the penalties imposed by these nets, but each Escape attempt deals @Damage[3d6[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage to the creature from the nets' barbs, regardless of whether the attempt succeeds. When the levaloch battalion is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage and takes a –5-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it Escapes.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1}, and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it Escapes.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2}, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} until it Escapes.

action->Merciless Tridents.name: Merciless Tridents
action->Merciless Tridents.description:
The levalochs make coordinated melee attacks with their tridents. Each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:37|basic|traits:magical,unholy] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[2d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus vicious cruelty

2 @Damage[(4d8+10)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus vicious cruelty

3 @Damage[(6d8+12)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage plus vicious cruelty

action->Vicious Cruelty.name: Vicious Cruelty
action->Vicious Cruelty.description: A creature that has the clumsy, enfeebled, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} condition takes an additional 1d6 precision damage from Merciless Tridents (2d6 precision damage if the levalochs take 2 or 3 actions).

Vinculatti.name: Vinculatti
Vinculatti.publicNotes: Warden devils exist to capture and imprison mortals, whether as jailers in Hell or hunters summoned by diabolists. Vinculattis exercise little discretion and show less interest in a prisoner's sentence or condition. Warden devils take cruel satisfaction in heaping psychological distress on their charges, enumerating the pleasures a prisoner is now denied.
Vinculatti.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->Fearsense 60 feet.name: Fearsense 60 feet
action->Fearsense 60 feet.description: The vinculatti is aware of all frightened creatures within the listed range.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Jingling Approach.name: Jingling Approach
action->Jingling Approach.description: When a creature rolls Perception for its initiative in combat against the vinculatti or succeeds at a Perception check against the vinculatti's Stealth DC, it must attempt a @Check[will|dc:27] save. On a failure, it's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

action->No Liberation.name: No Liberation
action->No Liberation.description:
Trigger A creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by the vinculatti attempts to Escape

Effect The triggering creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to the check to Escape.

action->Master of Manacles.name: Master of Manacles
action->Master of Manacles.description: The vinculatti's manacles can grapple or restrain any number of creatures within the devil's reach. They can apply the nonlethal trait to Constrict when damaging foes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by their manacles.

action->Prisoner Transfer.name: Prisoner Transfer
action->Prisoner Transfer.description:
Requirements A Large or smaller creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by the vinculatti's mandibles

Effect The vinculatti moves the creature to their manacles, freeing their mandibles to make attacks, and then Constricts the creature it moved.

action->To the Cells.name: To the Cells
action->To the Cells.description: The vinculatti Strides or Climbs up to half their Speed, moving any creatures it has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} the same distance in that direction.

action->Whispers of Despair.name: Whispers of Despair
action->Whispers of Despair.description:
Requirements A creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by the vinculatti

Effect The vinculatti taunts the creature about its captivity. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save. On a failure, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} on a critical failure). Regardless of the save's result, the target is temporarily immune to Whispers of Despair for 1 hour.

Visage of Asmodeus.name: Visage of Asmodeus
Visage of Asmodeus.descriptionHazard: A looming illusory image of the face of Asmodeus appears within the room, eyes glinting with malice at any who refuse to worship him.
Visage of Asmodeus.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:50] (master) to disrupt the rune projecting Asmodeus's face or @Check[religion|dc:48] (master) to refute the Asmodean faith; four total successes are required to disable the hazard
Visage of Asmodeus.reset: The trap deactivates 1 round after all creatures who don't worship Asmodeus either leave the room or are dead. It resets 1 round after that.
Visage of Asmodeus.routine:
(1 action; mental, visual) The illusory face of Asmodeus gazes at one creature in the area who doesn't worship Asmodeus. His eyes glint with diabolic power as that creature is overwhelmed by visions of the enormity of Hell. That creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:44|traits:mental,visual] saving throw. The hazard tends to target creatures who are attempting to disable the hazard. Taking damage from this hazard doesn't grant a flat check to end the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} condition.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[5d8[mental]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[10d8[mental]] damage and is confused for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[20d8[mental]] damage and is confused for 1 minute.

action->Menacing Laughter.name: Menacing Laughter
action->Menacing Laughter.description:
Trigger A creature who doesn't worship Asmodeus enters the room

Effect The booming voice of Asmodeus laughs cruelly at nonbelievers who would dare enter his highest temple on Golarion. Each creature who doesn't worship Asmodeus within 100 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:48|traits:auditory,emotion,fear,mental] save. The hazard then rolls initiative as Asmodeus' face appears in the middle of the vestry.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for as long as they're frightened.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 4}.

Vorrea Talminari.name: Vorrea Talminari
Vorrea Talminari.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. fear

weapon->Scimitar.name: Scimitar
weapon->Scimitar.description: This one-handed curved blade is sharp on one side.

armor->Reactive Mail (Major).name: Reactive Mail (Major)
armor->Reactive Mail (Major).description:
Often heavily battle scared from use, this +2 greater resilient chain mail is commonly issued to frontline soldiers who are tasked with battling through enemies to reach high-value targets across the battlefield.

Activate–Quick Spike r (manipulate, metal)

Frequency once per hour

Trigger A creature within 15 feet uses a manipulate action

Effect An iron spike launches from your armor at the target, dealing @Damage[7d8[piercing]] damage to the target (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic] save). If the target critically fails, the triggering action is disrupted.

equipment->Commandant's Scabbard.name: Commandant's Scabbard
equipment->Commandant's Scabbard.description:
Only the leader of an army could wear this diamond and ruby-encrusted scabbard that somehow always remains shiny no matter how terrible the conditions. While wearing the scabbard, you feel exceptionally powerful, and you gain a +3 item bonus to Athletics checks.

When you invest the scabbard, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. The commandant's scabbard holds one melee weapon of 1 Bulk or lighter. Whenever a weapon is drawn from the scabbard, it gains a +4 status bonus to damage for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.elbRo41iHweUkXtJ]{Effect: Commandant's Scabbard}

Activate—Commanding Draw 1 (fortune)

Frequency once per hour

Requirements You have a weapon sheathed in your commandant's scabbard

Effect You Interact to draw your weapon from your scabbard and Strike with it. On that Strike, you can roll twice and take the better result.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.dpoQrtDsUeEILBJ3]{Effect: Commanding Draw}

equipment->Fireproof Gloves (Greater).name: Fireproof Gloves (Greater)
equipment->Fireproof Gloves (Greater).description:
First developed by blacksmiths to move burning hot workpieces around, fireproof gloves then became popular with soldiers responsible for disabling bombs and magical traps. The thick tan gloves come up over the arm. When wearing these gloves, you gain fire resistance 10.

Activate—Release Heat 1 (concentrate, fire)

Frequency once per day

Requirements You have a free hand

Effect You take the heat that's built up in your gloves and discharge it onto an enemy. You deal @Damage[10d8[fire]] damage to one creature within reach (@Check[reflex|dc:31|basic|traits:concentrate,fire] save).

equipment->Magical Medal (Phoenix's Fire.name: Magical Medal (Phoenix's Fire)
equipment->Magical Medal (Phoenix's Fire.description:
Military leaders or heads of state award these special medals to commend exemplary performance by their top soldiers. They're typically worn on a special strip of fabric near the lapel, but soldiers from different countries sometimes wear them in other places. No matter how many magical medals you have, they collectively count as one invested item.

The phoenix on this gold medal is proud and fierce, and the medal features a border shaped like a flame. This medal is considered the highest honor in many countries and is awarded to those soldiers who have truly exemplified the ethics of their country through unbelievable service.

Activate—Phoenix's Sacrifice f (vitality)

Frequency once per day

Trigger Your @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{Dying} condition increases

Effect The phoenix bursts into flames. You lose the dying condition and regain @Damage[1[healing,vitality]|traits:vitality]{1 Hit Point}. Your @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded} value does not increase. You can use this action while @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}.

action->Supple Wrist.name: Supple Wrist
action->Supple Wrist.description: Vorrea Talminari gains an additional reaction each round that can only be used for A Foolish Move.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Fear.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Fear

action->A Foolish Move.name: A Foolish Move
action->A Foolish Move.description:
Frequency once per round

Trigger A creature critically misses Vorrea Talminari with a melee Strike or melee spell attack

Effect Vorrea makes an Arcane Strike against the triggering creature using @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.MPxbKoR54gkYkqLO]{Gouging Claw} but becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for her next turn.

action->Focus from Fear.name: Focus from Fear
action->Focus from Fear.description: Though fear weakens Vorrea's other abilities, it hones her offensive abilities. She applies only half her frightened condition value as a penalty to attack rolls (rounded down) and applies a status bonus to damage dealt by her Strikes equal to her frightened value.

action->Phoenix's Sacrifice.name: Phoenix's Sacrifice
action->Phoenix's Sacrifice.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger Your @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{Dying} condition increases

Effect The phoenix bursts into flames. You lose the dying condition and regain @Damage[1[healing,vitality]|traits:vitality]{1 Hit Point}. Your @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded} value does not increase. You can use this action while @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}.

action->Quick Spike.name: Quick Spike
action->Quick Spike.description:
Frequency once per hour

Trigger A creature within 15 feet uses a manipulate action

Effect An iron spike launches from your armor at the target, dealing @Damage[7d8[piercing]] damage to the target (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic] save). If the target critically fails, the triggering action is disrupted.

action->Arcane Strike.name: Arcane Strike
action->Arcane Strike.description: Vorrea Talminari Casts a Spell that deals damage. She then makes a melee Strike that counts as two attacks for the purposes of her multiple attack penalty but doesn't apply this penalty until after her Arcane Strike is concluded. If the spell requires an attack roll, use the Strike's results to determine the effects of both the Strike and the spell. If the spell requires a save, the target of the Strike rolls its saving throw normally, though if the Strike was a critical failure, the target is unaffected. Spells used through Arcane Strike can affect only a single target, even if they would normally affect an area or multiple targets.

action->Commanding Draw.name: Commanding Draw
action->Commanding Draw.description:
Frequency once per hour

Requirements You have a weapon sheathed in your @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Dk7cs7rj82ORgxFg]{Commandant's Scabbard}

Effect You Interact to draw your weapon from your scabbard and Strike with it. On that Strike, you can roll twice and take the better result.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.dpoQrtDsUeEILBJ3]{Effect: Commanding Draw}

action->Fancy Bladework.name: Fancy Bladework
action->Fancy Bladework.description: Vorrea attempts an Intimidation check to [[/act demoralize]] each enemy within 30 feet, without suffering penalties for not sharing a language. Regardless of the result, each creature is temporarily immune to Fancy Bladework for 1 minute.

action->Paracountess's Parry.name: Paracountess's Parry
action->Paracountess's Parry.description:
Requirements Vorrea Talminari just used Arcane Strike

Effect Vorrea raises the wide hilt of her cutlass in a defensive posture, surrounded by arcane energies to defend herself. She gains a +2 circumstance bonus to her AC and resistance 5 to physical damage (except silver) until the start of her next turn.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Ahn0Nqsus9vyPiN9]{Effect: Paracountess's Parry}

action->Release Heat.name: Release Heat
action->Release Heat.description:
Frequency once per day

Requirements You have a free hand

Effect You take the heat that's built up in your gloves and discharge it onto an enemy. You deal @Damage[10d8[fire]] damage to one creature within reach (@Check[reflex|dc:31|basic|traits:concentrate,fire] save).

action->Steady Spellcasting.name: Steady Spellcasting
action->Steady Spellcasting.description: If a reaction would disrupt Vorrea's spellcasting action, she attempts a @Check[flat|dc:15]. On a success, her action isn't disrupted.

action->Wind and Waves.name: Wind and Waves
action->Wind and Waves.description: Vorrea channels the elemental connection she has with the winds. She teleports to any square within 20 feet that's within reach of a creature, then makes a melee Strike. She can't use Wind and Waves again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Wind and Waves]]{1d4 rounds}.

Voulgarghas.name: Voulgarghas
Voulgarghas.publicNotes: Masters of possession, bdellavritras enact complex schemes through pawns in the mortal world. Even if expelled from their hosts, these fiends retain fearsome physical strength with bodies that bely their agility.
Voulgarghas.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->Frosty Whispers.name: Frosty Whispers
action->Frosty Whispers.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect Voulgarghas sings charming pleasantries from one of their four mouths to a single target within 30 feet. The target takes @Damage[10d6[cold]] damage and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:44] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature takes full damage, gains weakness 10 to cold, and is stupefied 1 for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, gains weakness 15 to cold, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 minute.

action->Mental Synergy.name: Mental Synergy
action->Mental Synergy.description:
Frequency once per minute

Requirements The bdellavritra is possessing a host using Mind Worm

Effect If the bdellavritra's next action is to Cast a Spell, they reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).

action->Mind Worm.name: Mind Worm
action->Mind Worm.description: The bdellavritra spends 10 minutes adjacent to an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} or willing living creature, then attempts to possess its body. This functions as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wU6hNzK8Yfqdmc8m]{Possession} heightened to 9th rank (@Check[will|dc:37] save), except that it has a duration of 1 month. If affected, the host receives new saving throws only at the start of each day. If the host dies, the bdellavritra must attempt a Fortitude save as normal but is only @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} on a failure (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 4} on a critical failure).

Wave of Damned Souls.name: Wave of Damned Souls
Wave of Damned Souls.descriptionHazard: A torrent of souls pulled from Hell pours through an archway, inflicting their personal suffering on all they touch.
Wave of Damned Souls.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:50] (master) or @Check[religion|dc:50] (master) to quell the souls' suffering; these checks must be attempted while adjacent to the arch, and four total successes are required to disable the haunt
Wave of Damned Souls.reset: After 1 minute, the wave of souls dries up, and the haunt deactivates. Over the course of 1 hour, planar energy rebuilds in the area, and the haunt resets.
Wave of Damned Souls.routine:
(1 action; divine, void) A seemingly endless flow of damned spirits emerges from the arch and washes down the street. Each creature within a @Template[type:line|distance:240] from the arch down the street takes @Damage[(4d10+24)[void]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:44|traits:divine,void] save. This line affects everyone within 20 feet of the ground.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and increases its doomed value by 1.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and increases its doomed value by 2.

action->Rising Tide of Screams.name: Rising Tide of Screams
action->Rising Tide of Screams.description:
Trigger A living creature comes within 15 feet of the arch

Effect The sounds of screams from the damned intensify as the arch begins to glow with planar energy. Each living creature within 60 feet of the arch must attempt a @Check[will|dc:48] saving throw. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 for the haunt's duration.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 4} and can't reduce its frightened condition below 2 for the duration of the haunt.

Window Alarm.name: Window Alarm
Window Alarm.descriptionHazard: Transparent runes around the window's edge trigger a silent alarm shortly after being opened by an unauthorized person.
Window Alarm.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:40] (master) to carefully scuff the faint runes around the window's edge

action->Signal the Keys.name: Signal the Keys
action->Signal the Keys.description:
Trigger The window is broken or is unlocked and opened without using one of the shop keys

Effect Each person carrying a shop key within 500 feet receives a mental image of the area 10 feet around the window for 1 minute.

action->Steady Spellcasting.name: Steady Spellcasting
action->Steady Spellcasting.description: If a reaction would disrupt the wretched diabolist's spellcasting action, the diabolist attempts a @Check[flat|dc:15]. On a success, the action isn't disrupted.

action->Wail of the Damned.name: Wail of the Damned
action->Wail of the Damned.description:
The diabolist laments their fate; Each living creature within 60 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:42|traits:auditory,divine,emotion,fear,mental] save.

Critical Success The creature is temporarily immune to this wretched diabolist's Wail of the Damned for 1 minute.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}.

weapon->Dagger.name: Dagger
weapon->Dagger.description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

equipment->Amulet of the Slumberer.name: Amulet of the Slumberer
equipment->Amulet of the Slumberer.description:
Companion Item to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.L6HpgX8EsBWCRJ3u]{The Eye of the Slumberer}.

Only one such amulet can exist at any given time, and the artifact can grow a new one in 10 minutes if the original is destroyed. The Eye's abilities that require a saving throw use the higher of the amulet wearer's spell DC or class DC.

consumable->Elixir of Life (Greater).name: Elixir of Life (Greater)
consumable->Elixir of Life (Greater).description:
Activate A (manipulate)

Elixirs of life accelerate a living creature's natural healing processes and immune system. Upon drinking this elixir, you regain @Damage[(7d6+18)[healing]] Hit Points and gain +2 item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-effects.Item.lPRuIRbu0rHBkoKY]{Effect: Elixir of Life}

action->Vortex.name: Vortex
action->Vortex.description:
20 feet.

While Xoxren wears the Amulet of the Slumberer, water in the area is difficult terrain for swimming creatures that don't have the water trait.

action->Consume Sacrifice.name: Consume Sacrifice
action->Consume Sacrifice.description:
Frequency once per turn

Requirements A sacrificial victim within 30 feet of Xoxren is tied to a sacrificial pillar

Effect Xoxren draws the final dregs of life from the victim. He gains @Damage[5d8[healing]] Hit Points and a +2 circumstance bonus to the DCs of his spells until the end of his next turn.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.UucZXsOc07MsMyPj]{Effect: Consume Sacrifice}

action->Unleash Tormented Psyche.name: Unleash Tormented Psyche
action->Unleash Tormented Psyche.description:
Trigger Xoxren's turn begins

Requirements Xoxren is in an encounter, he Cast a Spell on his previous turn, and he isn't stupefied

Effect While his psyche is unleashed, Xoxren's damaging spells deal an additional @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage to one target or @Damage[1d6[mental]] damage to each target if there are multiple targets. Xoxren's psyche remains unleashed for 2 rounds or until he falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}. Afterward, he is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 2 rounds.
